{{nihongo|'''Kanda Shrine'''|神田神社 (東白川村)}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Mino Province]].

link|Q11590647}} == Japanese content == {{神社 | 名称 = 神田神社 | 画像 = [[File:Kanda jinja Higashishirakawa.jpg|250px]]
鳥居と拝殿 | 位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|38|37|N|137|19|25|E|display=title,inline}} | 所在地 = {{jalink|岐阜県|Q131277}}{{jalink|加茂郡|Q1348656}}{{jalink|東白川村|Q1202765}}{{jalink|神土}}604 | 祭神= | 社格= {{jalink|式内小社|Q134917287|式内社}}（小）・旧{{jalink|郷社|Q134917316}}・{{jalink|金幣社|Q119929592}} | 別名 = {{jalink|白山妙大権現}}（{{jalink|白山妙理権現}}） | 創建= {{jalink|養老|Q1373061}}2年 | 本殿= | 例祭= 9月下旬 | 神事= | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Gifu|label=神田神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''神田神社'''（かんだじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|岐阜県|Q131277}}{{jalink|加茂郡|Q1348656}}{{jalink|東白川村|Q1202765}}{{jalink|神土}}（かんど）{{jalink|美濃国|Q864156}}{{jalink|加茂郡|Q1348656}}{{jalink|苗木藩|Q10863458|苗木領}}{{jalink|神土村}}にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}。{{jalink|美濃国|Q864156}}{{jalink|加茂郡|Q1348656|賀茂郡}}{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932|式内社}}の神田神社であるとされる。 毎年9月下旬の神田神社例大祭は、祭事のほか、獅子舞、御神酒の振る舞いなどが行われる。かつては{{jalink|山車|Q11470974}}の引き回しが行われていたが、1950年代に過疎化によって中止された。{{jalink|2008年|Q2004}}（{{jalink|平成|Q234510}}20年）に新たに山車が製作され、引き回しが復活した。 === 概略 === {{jalink|元正天皇|Q235343}}の{{jalink|養老|Q1373061}}2年（{{jalink|718年|Q23534}}）創建という。{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932}}では神田を「カムダ」と呼んでいたという。{{jalink|平安時代|Q193292}}中期以後は衰退したが、{{jalink|嘉慶 (日本)|Q1145253|嘉慶}}2年（{{jalink|1388年|Q6365}}）に再興修復され、{{jalink|神仏習合|Q2279088}}により'''白山妙大権現'''と改称する。 {{jalink|室町時代|Q334845}}に再び衰退したが、{{jalink|永禄|Q1143341}}元年（{{jalink|1558年|Q6601}}）に再興された。{{jalink|享保|Q1145048}}2年（{{jalink|1717年|Q6895}}）に建物を再建している。 {{jalink|明治|Q3125096}}元年（{{jalink|1868年|Q7717}}）、この地域は{{jalink|苗木藩|Q10863458}}により{{jalink|廃仏毀釈|Q702298}}が徹底的に行なわれ、白山妙大権現は神田神社に改称する。明治3年（{{jalink|1870年|Q7741}}）、{{jalink|神祇局}}により式内社の神田神社であるとされ、明治4年（{{jalink|1871年|Q7746}}）に苗木県の{{jalink|郷社|Q134917316}}、明治6年（{{jalink|1873年|Q7757}}）には岐阜県の郷社となる。 {{jalink|1952年|Q5272}}（昭和27年）7月1日、岐阜県神社庁より県神社庁長参向指定神社（{{jalink|金幣社|Q119929592}}）の指定（金13号）を受ける{{sfn|木村|1997|p=121}}。 === 祭神 === === 主神 === *{{jalink|イザナギ|Q813858|伊邪那岐命}} *{{jalink|イザナミ|Q682306|伊邪那美命}} *{{jalink|大国主|Q276944|大国主命}} *{{jalink|スクナビコナ|Q3275687|少彦名命}} *{{jalink|熊野権現|Q11568925|速玉男命}} === 合祀 === *{{jalink|サルタヒコ|Q3090037|猿田比古命}} *{{jalink|オオヤマツミ|Q386563|大山祇命}} *{{jalink|水分命}} *{{jalink|アマテラス||天照皇大神}} *{{jalink|菊理媛神|Q11619891|菊理比女命}} *{{jalink|仁徳天皇|Q313119|大雀命}} *{{jalink|カグツチ|Q1640658|火産霊命}} *{{jalink|誉田別命}}（{{jalink|八幡神|Q261637}}） *{{jalink|スサノオ|Q272993|須佐之男命}} === 所在地 === * 岐阜県加茂郡東白川村神土604 === 交通機関 === *{{jalink|濃飛乗合自動車|Q11566124|濃飛バス}}白川線「{{jalink|東白川村役場|Q109368715}}前」バス停下車。 **{{jalink|高山本線|Q1188357}}{{jalink|白川口駅|Q5358687}}より大明神行き === 参考文献 === * {{Cite book |和書 |editor=木村照 |title=岐阜県の主要神社巡り |publisher={{jalink|長良天神神社|Q3335060}} |date=1997 |ref={{SfnRef|木村|1997}}}} === 外部リンク === * [https://yaokami.jp/1212597/ 八百万の神] === 関連項目 === * {{jalink|越原神社}}[http://www.gifu-jinjacho.jp/syosai.php?shrno=522&shrname=%E2%96%A0%E8%B6%8A%E5%8E%9F%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE%E2%96%A0 神社庁] * {{jalink|五加神社}} === 脚注 === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} {{shinto-stub}} {{pref-stub|pref=岐阜県}} {{Buddhism-stub}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:かんたしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages]] [[Category:Autogenerated pages with jawiki interwikis, possibly accidentally overwritten]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Hakusan shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mino Province]] [[Category:東白川村の歴史]] {{jalink|式内小社|Q134917287|式内社}}（小）・旧{{jalink|郷社|Q134917316}}・{{jalink|金幣社|Q119929592}} | 別名 = {{jalink|白山妙大権現}}（{{jalink|白山妙理権現}}） | 創建= {{jalink|養老|Q1373061}}2年 | 本殿= | 例祭= 9月下旬 | 神事= | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Gifu|label=神田神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''神田神社'''（かんだじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|岐阜県|Q131277}}{{jalink|加茂郡|Q1348656}}{{jalink|東白川村|Q1202765}}{{jalink|神土}}（かんど）{{jalink|美濃国|Q864156}}{{jalink|加茂郡|Q1348656}}{{jalink|苗木藩|Q10863458|苗木領}}{{jalink|神土村}}にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}。{{jalink|美濃国|Q864156}}{{jalink|加茂郡|Q1348656|賀茂郡}}{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932|式内社}}の神田神社であるとされる。 毎年9月下旬の神田神社例大祭は、祭事のほか、獅子舞、御神酒の振る舞いなどが行われる。かつては{{jalink|山車|Q11470974}}の引き回しが行われていたが、1950年代に過疎化によって中止された。{{jalink|2008年|Q2004}}（{{jalink|平成|Q234510}}20年）に新たに山車が製作され、引き回しが復活した。 === 概略 === {{jalink|元正天皇|Q235343}}の{{jalink|養老|Q1373061}}2年（{{jalink|718年|Q23534}}）創建という。{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932}}では神田を「カムダ」と呼んでいたという。{{jalink|平安時代|Q193292}}中期以後は衰退したが、{{jalink|嘉慶 (日本)|Q1145253|嘉慶}}2年（{{jalink|1388年|Q6365}}）に再興修復され、{{jalink|神仏習合|Q2279088}}により'''白山妙大権現'''と改称する。 {{jalink|室町時代|Q334845}}に再び衰退したが、{{jalink|永禄|Q1143341}}元年（{{jalink|1558年|Q6601}}）に再興された。{{jalink|享保|Q1145048}}2年（{{jalink|1717年|Q6895}}）に建物を再建している。 {{jalink|明治|Q3125096}}元年（{{jalink|1868年|Q7717}}）、この地域は{{jalink|苗木藩|Q10863458}}により{{jalink|廃仏毀釈|Q702298}}が徹底的に行なわれ、白山妙大権現は神田神社に改称する。明治3年（{{jalink|1870年|Q7741}}）、{{jalink|神祇局}}により式内社の神田神社であるとされ、明治4年（{{jalink|1871年|Q7746}}）に苗木県の{{jalink|郷社|Q134917316}}、明治6年（{{jalink|1873年|Q7757}}）には岐阜県の郷社となる。 {{jalink|1952年|Q5272}}（昭和27年）7月1日、岐阜県神社庁より県神社庁長参向指定神社（{{jalink|金幣社|Q119929592}}）の指定（金13号）を受ける{{sfn|木村|1997|p=121}}。 === 祭神 === === 主神 === *{{jalink|イザナギ|Q813858|伊邪那岐命}} *{{jalink|イザナミ|Q682306|伊邪那美命}} *{{jalink|大国主|Q276944|大国主命}} *{{jalink|スクナビコナ|Q3275687|少彦名命}} *{{jalink|熊野権現|Q11568925|速玉男命}} === 合祀 === *{{jalink|サルタヒコ|Q3090037|猿田比古命}} *{{jalink|オオヤマツミ|Q386563|大山祇命}} *{{jalink|水分命}} *{{jalink|アマテラス||天照皇大神}} *{{jalink|菊理媛神|Q11619891|菊理比女命}} *{{jalink|仁徳天皇|Q313119|大雀命}} *{{jalink|カグツチ|Q1640658|火産霊命}} *{{jalink|誉田別命}}（{{jalink|八幡神|Q261637}}） *{{jalink|スサノオ|Q272993|須佐之男命}} === 所在地 === * 岐阜県加茂郡東白川村神土604 === 交通機関 === *{{jalink|濃飛乗合自動車|Q11566124|濃飛バス}}白川線「{{jalink|東白川村役場|Q109368715}}前」バス停下車。 **{{jalink|高山本線|Q1188357}}{{jalink|白川口駅|Q5358687}}より大明神行き === 参考文献 === * {{Cite book |和書 |editor=木村照 |title=岐阜県の主要神社巡り |publisher={{jalink|長良天神神社|Q3335060}} |date=1997 |ref={{SfnRef|木村|1997}}}} === 外部リンク === * [https://yaokami.jp/1212597/ 八百万の神] === 関連項目 === * {{jalink|越原神社}}[http://www.gifu-jinjacho.jp/syosai.php?shrno=522&shrname=%E2%96%A0%E8%B6%8A%E5%8E%9F%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE%E2%96%A0 神社庁] * {{jalink|五加神社}} === 脚注 === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} {{shinto-stub}} {{pref-stub|pref=岐阜県}} {{Buddhism-stub}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:かんたしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages]] [[Category:Pages]] link|Q11590647}} == Japanese content == {{神社 | 名称 = 神田神社 | 画像 = [[File:Kanda jinja Higashishirakawa.jpg|250px]]
鳥居と拝殿 | 位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|38|37|N|137|19|25|E|display=title,inline}} | 所在地 = {{jalink|岐阜県|Q131277}}{{jalink|加茂郡|Q1348656}}{{jalink|東白川村|Q1202765}}{{jalink|神土}}604 | 祭神= | 社格= {{jalink|式内小社|Q134917287|式内社}}（小）・旧{{jalink|郷社|Q134917316}}・{{jalink|金幣社|Q119929592}} | 別名 = {{jalink|白山妙大権現}}（{{jalink|白山妙理権現}}） | 創建= {{jalink|養老|Q1373061}}2年 | 本殿= | 例祭= 9月下旬 | 神事= | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Gifu|label=神田神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''神田神社'''（かんだじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|岐阜県|Q131277}}{{jalink|加茂郡|Q1348656}}{{jalink|東白川村|Q1202765}}{{jalink|神土}}（かんど）{{jalink|美濃国|Q864156}}{{jalink|加茂郡|Q1348656}}{{jalink|苗木藩|Q10863458|苗木領}}{{jalink|神土村}}にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}。{{jalink|美濃国|Q864156}}{{jalink|加茂郡|Q1348656|賀茂郡}}{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932|式内社}}の神田神社であるとされる。 毎年9月下旬の神田神社例大祭は、祭事のほか、獅子舞、御神酒の振る舞いなどが行われる。かつては{{jalink|山車|Q11470974}}の引き回しが行われていたが、1950年代に過疎化によって中止された。{{jalink|2008年|Q2004}}（{{jalink|平成|Q234510}}20年）に新たに山車が製作され、引き回しが復活した。 === 概略 === {{jalink|元正天皇|Q235343}}の{{jalink|養老|Q1373061}}2年（{{jalink|718年|Q23534}}）創建という。{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932}}では神田を「カムダ」と呼んでいたという。{{jalink|平安時代|Q193292}}中期以後は衰退したが、{{jalink|嘉慶 (日本)|Q1145253|嘉慶}}2年（{{jalink|1388年|Q6365}}）に再興修復され、{{jalink|神仏習合|Q2279088}}により'''白山妙大権現'''と改称する。 {{jalink|室町時代|Q334845}}に再び衰退したが、{{jalink|永禄|Q1143341}}元年（{{jalink|1558年|Q6601}}）に再興された。{{jalink|享保|Q1145048}}2年（{{jalink|1717年|Q6895}}）に建物を再建している。 {{jalink|明治|Q3125096}}元年（{{jalink|1868年|Q7717}}）、この地域は{{jalink|苗木藩|Q10863458}}により{{jalink|廃仏毀釈|Q702298}}が徹底的に行なわれ、白山妙大権現は神田神社に改称する。明治3年（{{jalink|1870年|Q7741}}）、{{jalink|神祇局}}により式内社の神田神社であるとされ、明治4年（{{jalink|1871年|Q7746}}）に苗木県の{{jalink|郷社|Q134917316}}、明治6年（{{jalink|1873年|Q7757}}）には岐阜県の郷社となる。 {{jalink|1952年|Q5272}}（昭和27年）7月1日、岐阜県神社庁より県神社庁長参向指定神社（{{jalink|金幣社|Q119929592}}）の指定（金13号）を受ける{{sfn|木村|1997|p=121}}。 === 祭神 === === 主神 === *{{jalink|イザナギ|Q813858|伊邪那岐命}} *{{jalink|イザナミ|Q682306|伊邪那美命}} *{{jalink|大国主|Q276944|大国主命}} *{{jalink|スクナビコナ|Q3275687|少彦名命}} *{{jalink|熊野権現|Q11568925|速玉男命}} === 合祀 === *{{jalink|サルタヒコ|Q3090037|猿田比古命}} *{{jalink|オオヤマツミ|Q386563|大山祇命}} *{{jalink|水分命}} *{{jalink|アマテラス||天照皇大神}} *{{jalink|菊理媛神|Q11619891|菊理比女命}} *{{jalink|仁徳天皇|Q313119|大雀命}} *{{jalink|カグツチ|Q1640658|火産霊命}} *{{jalink|誉田別命}}（{{jalink|八幡神|Q261637}}） *{{jalink|スサノオ|Q272993|須佐之男命}} === 所在地 === * 岐阜県加茂郡東白川村神土604 === 交通機関 === *{{jalink|濃飛乗合自動車|Q11566124|濃飛バス}}白川線「{{jalink|東白川村役場|Q109368715}}前」バス停下車。 **{{jalink|高山本線|Q1188357}}{{jalink|白川口駅|Q5358687}}より大明神行き === 参考文献 === * {{Cite book |和書 |editor=木村照 |title=岐阜県の主要神社巡り |publisher={{jalink|長良天神神社|Q3335060}} |date=1997 |ref={{SfnRef|木村|1997}}}} === 外部リンク === * [https://yaokami.jp/1212597/ 八百万の神] === 関連項目 === * {{jalink|越原神社}}[http://www.gifu-jinjacho.jp/syosai.php?shrno=522&shrname=%E2%96%A0%E8%B6%8A%E5%8E%9F%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE%E2%96%A0 神社庁] * {{jalink|五加神社}} === 脚注 === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} {{shinto-stub}} link|Q11590647}} == Japanese content == {{神社 | 名称 = 神田神社 | 画像 = [[File:Kanda jinja Higashishirakawa.jpg|250px]]
鳥居と拝殿 | 位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|38|37|N|137|19|25|E|display=title,inline}} | 所在地 = {{jalink|岐阜県|Q131277}}{{jalink|加茂郡|Q1348656}}{{jalink|東白川村|Q1202765}}{{jalink|神土}}604 | 祭神= | 社格= {{jalink|式内小社|Q134917287|式内社}}（小）・旧{{jalink|郷社|Q134917316}}・{{jalink|金幣社|Q119929592}} | 別名 = {{jalink|白山妙大権現}}（{{jalink|白山妙理権現}}） | 創建= {{jalink|養老|Q1373061}}2年 | 本殿= | 例祭= 9月下旬 | 神事= | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Gifu|label=神田神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''神田神社'''（かんだじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|岐阜県|Q131277}}{{jalink|加茂郡|Q1348656}}{{jalink|東白川村|Q1202765}}{{jalink|神土}}（かんど）{{jalink|美濃国|Q864156}}{{jalink|加茂郡|Q1348656}}{{jalink|苗木藩|Q10863458|苗木領}}{{jalink|神土村}}にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}。{{jalink|美濃国|Q864156}}{{jalink|加茂郡|Q1348656|賀茂郡}}{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932|式内社}}の神田神社であるとされる。 毎年9月下旬の神田神社例大祭は、祭事のほか、獅子舞、御神酒の振る舞いなどが行われる。かつては{{jalink|山車|Q11470974}}の引き回しが行われていたが、1950年代に過疎化によって中止された。{{jalink|2008年|Q2004}}（{{jalink|平成|Q234510}}20年）に新たに山車が製作され、引き回しが復活した。 === 概略 === {{jalink|元正天皇|Q235343}}の{{jalink|養老|Q1373061}}2年（{{jalink|718年|Q23534}}）創建という。{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932}}では神田を「カムダ」と呼んでいたという。{{jalink|平安時代|Q193292}}中期以後は衰退したが、{{jalink|嘉慶 (日本)|Q1145253|嘉慶}}2年（{{jalink|1388年|Q6365}}）に再興修復され、{{jalink|神仏習合|Q2279088}}により'''白山妙大権現'''と改称する。 {{jalink|室町時代|Q334845}}に再び衰退したが、{{jalink|永禄|Q1143341}}元年（{{jalink|1558年|Q6601}}）に再興された。{{jalink|享保|Q1145048}}2年（{{jalink|1717年|Q6895}}）に建物を再建している。 {{jalink|明治|Q3125096}}元年（{{jalink|1868年|Q7717}}）、この地域は{{jalink|苗木藩|Q10863458}}により{{jalink|廃仏毀釈|Q702298}}が徹底的に行なわれ、白山妙大権現は神田神社に改称する。明治3年（{{jalink|1870年|Q7741}}）、{{jalink|神祇局}}により式内社の神田神社であるとされ、明治4年（{{jalink|1871年|Q7746}}）に苗木県の{{jalink|郷社|Q134917316}}、明治6年（{{jalink|1873年|Q7757}}）には岐阜県の郷社となる。 {{jalink|1952年|Q5272}}（昭和27年）7月1日、岐阜県神社庁より県神社庁長参向指定神社（{{jalink|金幣社|Q119929592}}）の指定（金13号）を受ける{{sfn|木村|1997|p=121}}。 === 祭神 === === 主神 === *{{jalink|イザナギ|Q813858|伊邪那岐命}} *{{jalink|イザナミ|Q682306|伊邪那美命}} *{{jalink|大国主|Q276944|大国主命}} *{{jalink|スクナビコナ|Q3275687|少彦名命}} *{{jalink|熊野権現|Q11568925|速玉男命}} === 合祀 === *{{jalink|サルタヒコ|Q3090037|猿田比古命}} *{{jalink|オオヤマツミ|Q386563|大山祇命}} *{{jalink|水分命}} *{{jalink|アマテラス||天照皇大神}} *{{jalink|菊理媛神|Q11619891|菊理比女命}} *{{jalink|仁徳天皇|Q313119|大雀命}} *{{jalink|カグツチ|Q1640658|火産霊命}} *{{jalink|誉田別命}}（{{jalink|八幡神|Q261637}}） *{{jalink|スサノオ|Q272993|須佐之男命}} === 所在地 === * 岐阜県加茂郡東白川村神土604 === 交通機関 === *{{jalink|濃飛乗合自動車|Q11566124|濃飛バス}}白川線「{{jalink|東白川村役場|Q109368715}}前」バス停下車。 **{{jalink|高山本線|Q1188357}}{{jalink|白川口駅|Q5358687}}より大明神行き === 参考文献 === * {{Cite book |和書 |editor=木村照 |title=岐阜県の主要神社巡り |publisher={{jalink|長良天神神社|Q3335060}} |date=1997 |ref={{SfnRef|木村|1997}}}} === 外部リンク === * [https://yaokami.jp/1212597/ 八百万の神] === 関連項目 === * {{jalink|越原神社}}[http://www.gifu-jinjacho.jp/syosai.php?shrno=522&shrname=%E2%96%A0%E8%B6%8A%E5%8E%9F%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE%E2%96%A0 神社庁] * {{jalink|五加神社}} === 脚注 === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} {{shinto-stub}} link|Q11590647}} == Japanese content == {{神社 | 名称 = 神田神社 | 画像 = [[File:Kanda jinja Higashishirakawa.jpg|250px]]
鳥居と拝殿 | 位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|38|37|N|137|19|25|E|display=title,inline}} | 所在地 = {{jalink|岐阜県|Q131277}}{{jalink|加茂郡|Q1348656}}{{jalink|東白川村|Q1202765}}{{jalink|神土}}604 | 祭神= | 社格= {{jalink|式内小社|Q134917287|式内社}}（小）・旧{{jalink|郷社|Q134917316}}・{{jalink|金幣社|Q119929592}} | 別名 = {{jalink|白山妙大権現}}（{{jalink|白山妙理権現}}） | 創建= {{jalink|養老|Q1373061}}2年 | 本殿= | 例祭= 9月下旬 | 神事= | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Gifu|label=神田神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''神田神社'''（かんだじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|岐阜県|Q131277}}{{jalink|加茂郡|Q1348656}}{{jalink|東白川村|Q1202765}}{{jalink|神土}}（かんど）{{jalink|美濃国|Q864156}}{{jalink|加茂郡|Q1348656}}{{jalink|苗木藩|Q10863458|苗木領}}{{jalink|神土村}}にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}。{{jalink|美濃国|Q864156}}{{jalink|加茂郡|Q1348656|賀茂郡}}{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932|式内社}}の神田神社であるとされる。 毎年9月下旬の神田神社例大祭は、祭事のほか、獅子舞、御神酒の振る舞いなどが行われる。かつては{{jalink|山車|Q11470974}}の引き回しが行われていたが、1950年代に過疎化によって中止された。{{jalink|2008年|Q2004}}（{{jalink|平成|Q234510}}20年）に新たに山車が製作され、引き回しが復活した。 === 概略 === {{jalink|元正天皇|Q235343}}の{{jalink|養老|Q1373061}}2年（{{jalink|718年|Q23534}}）創建という。{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932}}では神田を「カムダ」と呼んでいたという。{{jalink|平安時代|Q193292}}中期以後は衰退したが、{{jalink|嘉慶 (日本)|Q1145253|嘉慶}}2年（{{jalink|1388年|Q6365}}）に再興修復され、{{jalink|神仏習合|Q2279088}}により'''白山妙大権現'''と改称する。 {{jalink|室町時代|Q334845}}に再び衰退したが、{{jalink|永禄|Q1143341}}元年（{{jalink|1558年|Q6601}}）に再興された。{{jalink|享保|Q1145048}}2年（{{jalink|1717年|Q6895}}）に建物を再建している。 {{jalink|明治|Q3125096}}元年（{{jalink|1868年|Q7717}}）、この地域は{{jalink|苗木藩|Q10863458}}により{{jalink|廃仏毀釈|Q702298}}が徹底的に行なわれ、白山妙大権現は神田神社に改称する。明治3年（{{jalink|1870年|Q7741}}）、{{jalink|神祇局}}により式内社の神田神社であるとされ、明治4年（{{jalink|1871年|Q7746}}）に苗木県の{{jalink|郷社|Q134917316}}、明治6年（{{jalink|1873年|Q7757}}）には岐阜県の郷社となる。 {{jalink|1952年|Q5272}}（昭和27年）7月1日、岐阜県神社庁より県神社庁長参向指定神社（{{jalink|金幣社|Q119929592}}）の指定（金13号）を受ける{{sfn|木村|1997|p=121}}。 === 祭神 === === 主神 === *{{jalink|イザナギ|Q813858|伊邪那岐命}} *{{jalink|イザナミ|Q682306|伊邪那美命}} *{{jalink|大国主|Q276944|大国主命}} *{{jalink|スクナビコナ|Q3275687|少彦名命}} *{{jalink|熊野権現|Q11568925|速玉男命}} === 合祀 === *{{jalink|サルタヒコ|Q3090037|猿田比古命}} *{{jalink|オオヤマツミ|Q386563|大山祇命}} *{{jalink|水分命}} *{{jalink|アマテラス||天照皇大神}} *{{jalink|菊理媛神|Q11619891|菊理比女命}} *{{jalink|仁徳天皇|Q313119|大雀命}} *{{jalink|カグツチ|Q1640658|火産霊命}} *{{jalink|誉田別命}}（{{jalink|八幡神|Q261637}}） *{{jalink|スサノオ|Q272993|須佐之男命}} === 所在地 === * 岐阜県加茂郡東白川村神土604 === 交通機関 === *{{jalink|濃飛乗合自動車|Q11566124|濃飛バス}}白川線「{{jalink|東白川村役場|Q109368715}}前」バス停下車。 **{{jalink|高山本線|Q1188357}}{{jalink|白川口駅|Q5358687}}より大明神行き === 参考文献 === * {{Cite book |和書 |editor=木村照 |title=岐阜県の主要神社巡り |publisher={{jalink|長良天神神社|Q3335060}} |date=1997 |ref={{SfnRef|木村|1997}}}} === 外部リンク === * [https://yaokami.jp/1212597/ 八百万の神] === 関連項目 === * {{jalink|越原神社}}[http://www.gifu-jinjacho.jp/syosai.php?shrno=522&shrname=%E2%96%A0%E8%B6%8A%E5%8E%9F%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE%E2%96%A0 神社庁] * {{jalink|五加神社}} === 脚注 === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} {{shinto-stub}} link|Q11590647}} == Japanese content == {{神社 | 名称 = 神田神社 | 画像 = [[File:Kanda jinja Higashishirakawa.jpg|250px]]
鳥居と拝殿 | 位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|38|37|N|137|19|25|E|display=title,inline}} | 所在地 = {{jalink|岐阜県|Q131277}}{{jalink|加茂郡|Q1348656}}{{jalink|東白川村|Q1202765}}{{jalink|神土}}604 | 祭神= | 社格= {{jalink|式内小社|Q134917287|式内社}}（小）・旧{{jalink|郷社|Q134917316}}・{{jalink|金幣社|Q119929592}} | 別名 = {{jalink|白山妙大権現}}（{{jalink|白山妙理権現}}） | 創建= {{jalink|養老|Q1373061}}2年 | 本殿= | 例祭= 9月下旬 | 神事= | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Gifu|label=神田神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''神田神社'''（かんだじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|岐阜県|Q131277}}{{jalink|加茂郡|Q1348656}}{{jalink|東白川村|Q1202765}}{{jalink|神土}}（かんど）{{jalink|美濃国|Q864156}}{{jalink|加茂郡|Q1348656}}{{jalink|苗木藩|Q10863458|苗木領}}{{jalink|神土村}}にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}。{{jalink|美濃国|Q864156}}{{jalink|加茂郡|Q1348656|賀茂郡}}{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932|式内社}}の神田神社であるとされる。 毎年9月下旬の神田神社例大祭は、祭事のほか、獅子舞、御神酒の振る舞いなどが行われる。かつては{{jalink|山車|Q11470974}}の引き回しが行われていたが、1950年代に過疎化によって中止された。{{jalink|2008年|Q2004}}（{{jalink|平成|Q234510}}20年）に新たに山車が製作され、引き回しが復活した。 === 概略 === {{jalink|元正天皇|Q235343}}の{{jalink|養老|Q1373061}}2年（{{jalink|718年|Q23534}}）創建という。{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932}}では神田を「カムダ」と呼んでいたという。{{jalink|平安時代|Q193292}}中期以後は衰退したが、{{jalink|嘉慶 (日本)|Q1145253|嘉慶}}2年（{{jalink|1388年|Q6365}}）に再興修復され、{{jalink|神仏習合|Q2279088}}により'''白山妙大権現'''と改称する。 {{jalink|室町時代|Q334845}}に再び衰退したが、{{jalink|永禄|Q1143341}}元年（{{jalink|1558年|Q6601}}）に再興された。{{jalink|享保|Q1145048}}2年（{{jalink|1717年|Q6895}}）に建物を再建している。 {{jalink|明治|Q3125096}}元年（{{jalink|1868年|Q7717}}）、この地域は{{jalink|苗木藩|Q10863458}}により{{jalink|廃仏毀釈|Q702298}}が徹底的に行なわれ、白山妙大権現は神田神社に改称する。明治3年（{{jalink|1870年|Q7741}}）、{{jalink|神祇局}}により式内社の神田神社であるとされ、明治4年（{{jalink|1871年|Q7746}}）に苗木県の{{jalink|郷社|Q134917316}}、明治6年（{{jalink|1873年|Q7757}}）には岐阜県の郷社となる。 {{jalink|1952年|Q5272}}（昭和27年）7月1日、岐阜県神社庁より県神社庁長参向指定神社（{{jalink|金幣社|Q119929592}}）の指定（金13号）を受ける{{sfn|木村|1997|p=121}}。 === 祭神 === === 主神 === *{{jalink|イザナギ|Q813858|伊邪那岐命}} *{{jalink|イザナミ|Q682306|伊邪那美命}} *{{jalink|大国主|Q276944|大国主命}} *{{jalink|スクナビコナ|Q3275687|少彦名命}} *{{jalink|熊野権現|Q11568925|速玉男命}} === 合祀 === *{{jalink|サルタヒコ|Q3090037|猿田比古命}} *{{jalink|オオヤマツミ|Q386563|大山祇命}} *{{jalink|水分命}} *{{jalink|アマテラス||天照皇大神}} *{{jalink|菊理媛神|Q11619891|菊理比女命}} *{{jalink|仁徳天皇|Q313119|大雀命}} *{{jalink|カグツチ|Q1640658|火産霊命}} *{{jalink|誉田別命}}（{{jalink|八幡神|Q261637}}） *{{jalink|スサノオ|Q272993|須佐之男命}} === 所在地 === * 岐阜県加茂郡東白川村神土604 === 交通機関 === *{{jalink|濃飛乗合自動車|Q11566124|濃飛バス}}白川線「{{jalink|東白川村役場|Q109368715}}前」バス停下車。 **{{jalink|高山本線|Q1188357}}{{jalink|白川口駅|Q5358687}}より大明神行き === 参考文献 === * {{Cite book |和書 |editor=木村照 |title=岐阜県の主要神社巡り |publisher={{jalink|長良天神神社|Q3335060}} |date=1997 |ref={{SfnRef|木村|1997}}}} === 外部リンク === * [https://yaokami.jp/1212597/ 八百万の神] === 関連項目 === * {{jalink|越原神社}}[http://www.gifu-jinjacho.jp/syosai.php?shrno=522&shrname=%E2%96%A0%E8%B6%8A%E5%8E%9F%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE%E2%96%A0 神社庁] * {{jalink|五加神社}} === 脚注 === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} {{shinto-stub}} {{pref-stub|pref=岐阜県}} {{Buddhism-stub}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:かんたしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kanda Shrine (Q11590647)]][[Category:Shikinaisha]] [[Category:Pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Hakusan shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mino Province]] (日本)|Q1145253|嘉慶}}2年（{{jalink|1388年|Q6365}}）に再興修復され、{{jalink|神仏習合|Q2279088}}により'''白山妙大権現'''と改称する。 {{jalink|室町時代|Q334845}}に再び衰退したが、{{jalink|永禄|Q1143341}}元年（{{jalink|1558年|Q6601}}）に再興された。{{jalink|享保|Q1145048}}2年（{{jalink|1717年|Q6895}}）に建物を再建している。 {{jalink|明治|Q3125096}}元年（{{jalink|1868年|Q7717}}）、この地域は{{jalink|苗木藩|Q10863458}}により{{jalink|廃仏毀釈|Q702298}}が徹底的に行なわれ、白山妙大権現は神田神社に改称する。明治3年（{{jalink|1870年|Q7741}}）、{{jalink|神祇局}}により式内社の神田神社であるとされ、明治4年（{{jalink|1871年|Q7746}}）に苗木県の{{jalink|郷社|Q134917316}}、明治6年（{{jalink|1873年|Q7757}}）には岐阜県の郷社となる。 {{jalink|1952年|Q5272}}（昭和27年）7月1日、岐阜県神社庁より県神社庁長参向指定神社（{{jalink|金幣社|Q119929592}}）の指定（金13号）を受ける{{sfn|木村|1997|p=121}}。 === 祭神 === === 主神 === *{{jalink|イザナギ|Q813858|伊邪那岐命}} *{{jalink|イザナミ|Q682306|伊邪那美命}} *{{jalink|大国主|Q276944|大国主命}} *{{jalink|スクナビコナ|Q3275687|少彦名命}} *{{jalink|熊野権現|Q11568925|速玉男命}} === 合祀 === *{{jalink|サルタヒコ|Q3090037|猿田比古命}} *{{jalink|オオヤマツミ|Q386563|大山祇命}} *{{jalink|水分命}} *{{jalink|アマテラス||天照皇大神}} *{{jalink|菊理媛神|Q11619891|菊理比女命}} *{{jalink|仁徳天皇|Q313119|大雀命}} *{{jalink|カグツチ|Q1640658|火産霊命}} *{{jalink|誉田別命}}（{{jalink|八幡神|Q261637}}） *{{jalink|スサノオ|Q272993|須佐之男命}} === 所在地 === * 岐阜県加茂郡東白川村神土604 === 交通機関 === *{{jalink|濃飛乗合自動車|Q11566124|濃飛バス}}白川線「{{jalink|東白川村役場|Q109368715}}前」バス停下車。 **{{jalink|高山本線|Q1188357}}{{jalink|白川口駅|Q5358687}}より大明神行き === 参考文献 === * {{Cite book |和書 |editor=木村照 |title=岐阜県の主要神社巡り |publisher={{jalink|長良天神神社|Q3335060}} |date=1997 |ref={{SfnRef|木村|1997}}}} === 外部リンク === * [https://yaokami.jp/1212597/ 八百万の神] === 関連項目 === * {{jalink|越原神社}}[http://www.gifu-jinjacho.jp/syosai.php?shrno=522&shrname=%E2%96%A0%E8%B6%8A%E5%8E%9F%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE%E2%96%A0 神社庁] * {{jalink|五加神社}} === 脚注 === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} {{shinto-stub}} {{pref-stub|pref=岐阜県}} {{Buddhism-stub}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:かんたしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kanda Shrine (Q11590647)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kanda Shrine (Q11590647)]][[Category:Pages]] [[Category:Pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Hakusan shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] Torii and haiden | 位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|38|37|N|137|19|25|E|display=title,inline}} | 所在地 = {{jalink|Gifu Prefecture|Q131277}}{{jalink|Kamo District, Gifu|Q1348656}}{{jalink|Higashishirakawa|Q1202765}}{{jalink|Kando}}604 | 祭神= | 社格= {{jalink|Shikinai Shosha|Q134917287|Shikinaisha}}（small）・former {{jalink|Gōsha|Q134917316}}・{{jalink|Kinpei-sha|Q119929592}} | 別名 = {{jalink|Hakusan Myōdaigongen}}（{{jalink|Hakusan Myōri Gongen}}） | 創建= {{jalink|Yōrō|Q1373061}} 2 | 例祭= late September | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Gifu|label=Kanda Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Kanda Shrine'''（かんだじんじゃ）is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} in {{jalink|Kando}}, {{jalink|Higashishirakawa|Q1202765}}, {{jalink|Kamo District, Gifu|Q1348656}}, {{jalink|Gifu Prefecture|Q131277}}. It is regarded as Kanda Shrine listed in the {{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932}} for {{jalink|Mino Province|Q864156}} Kamo District. The annual grand festival in late September includes ritual ceremonies, lion dance, and ceremonial sake distribution. Floats used to be pulled through the village, but this stopped in the 1950s due to depopulation. In {{jalink|2008|Q2004}}（{{jalink|Heisei|Q234510}} 20）, a new float was built and the procession revived. === Overview === It is said to have been founded in {{jalink|Yōrō|Q1373061}} 2 ({{jalink|718|Q23534}}). In the Engishiki period, "Kanda" is said to have been read as "Kamuda." It declined after the mid-Heian period, but was restored in Kakei 2 ({{jalink|1388|Q6365}}), and through {{jalink|Shinbutsu-shūgō|Q2279088}} was renamed '''Hakusan Myōdaigongen'''. It declined again in the Muromachi period, but was restored in Eiroku 1 ({{jalink|1558|Q6601}}). Buildings were rebuilt in Kyōhō 2 ({{jalink|1717|Q6895}}). In Meiji 1 ({{jalink|1868|Q7717}}), this area underwent thorough {{jalink|haibutsu kishaku|Q702298}} under the {{jalink|Naegi Domain|Q10863458}}, and Hakusan Myōdaigongen was renamed Kanda Shrine. In Meiji 3 ({{jalink|1870|Q7741}}), the {{jalink|Department of Divinities}} recognized it as the Shikinaisha Kanda Shrine. In Meiji 4 ({{jalink|1871|Q7746}}) it became a gōsha of Naegi Prefecture, and in Meiji 6 ({{jalink|1873|Q7757}}) a gōsha of Gifu Prefecture. On July 1, 1952 (Shōwa 27), it was designated by Gifu Prefectural Shrine Office as a shrine receiving regular offerings (Kinpei-sha designation No. 13){{sfn|木村|1997|p=121}}. === Enshrined deities === === Main deities === *{{jalink|Izanagi|Q813858|Izanagi-no-Mikoto}} *{{jalink|Izanami|Q682306|Izanami-no-Mikoto}} *{{jalink|Ōkuninushi|Q276944|Ōkuninushi-no-Mikoto}} *{{jalink|Sukunabikona|Q3275687|Sukunahikona-no-Mikoto}} *{{jalink|Kumano Gongen|Q11568925|Hayatama-no-o-no-Mikoto}} === Merged enshrined deities === *{{jalink|Sarutahiko|Q3090037|Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto}} *{{jalink|Ōyamatsumi|Q386563|Ōyamazumi-no-Mikoto}} *{{jalink|Mizuwake-no-Mikoto}} *{{jalink|Amaterasu||Amaterasu-Ōmikami}} *{{jalink|Kukurihime|Q11619891|Kukurihime-no-Mikoto}} *{{jalink|Emperor Nintoku|Q313119|Ōsazaki-no-Mikoto}} *{{jalink|Kagutsuchi|Q1640658|Homusubi-no-Mikoto}} *{{jalink|Homuda-wake-no-Mikoto}}（{{jalink|Hachiman deity|Q261637}}） *{{jalink|Susanoo|Q272993|Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} === Address === * Gifu Prefecture, Kamo District, Higashishirakawa Village, Kando 604 === Transportation === * {{jalink|Nōhi Bus|Q11566124|Nōhi Bus}} Shirakawa Line, get off at "{{jalink|Higashishirakawa Village Office|Q109368715}} Mae". ** From {{jalink|Shirakawa-guchi Station|Q5358687}} on the {{jalink|Takayama Main Line|Q1188357}}, bus bound for Daimyōjin. === References === * {{Cite book |和書 |editor=Teru Kimura |title=Major Shrine Tour in Gifu Prefecture |publisher={{jalink|Nagaranoten Jinja|Q3335060}} |date=1997 |ref={{SfnRef|木村|1997}}}} === External links === * [https://yaokami.jp/1212597/ Yaokami] === Related items === * {{jalink|Koshihara Shrine}}[http://www.gifu-jinjacho.jp/syosai.php?shrno=522&shrname=%E2%96%A0%E8%B6%8A%E5%8E%9F%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE%E2%96%A0 Shrine Office] * {{jalink|Goka Shrine}} === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} Torii and haiden | 位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|38|37|N|137|19|25|E|display=title,inline}} | 所在地 = {{jalink|Gifu Prefecture|Q131277}}{{jalink|Kamo District, Gifu|Q1348656}}{{jalink|Higashishirakawa|Q1202765}}{{jalink|Kando}}604 | 祭神= | 社格= {{jalink|Shikinai Shosha|Q134917287|Shikinaisha}}（small）・former {{jalink|Gōsha|Q134917316}}・{{jalink|Kinpei-sha|Q119929592}} | 別名 = {{jalink|Hakusan Myōdaigongen}}（{{jalink|Hakusan Myōri Gongen}}） | 創建= {{jalink|Yōrō|Q1373061}} 2 | 例祭= late September | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Gifu|label=Kanda Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Kanda Shrine'''（かんだじんじゃ）is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} in {{jalink|Kando}}, {{jalink|Higashishirakawa|Q1202765}}, {{jalink|Kamo District, Gifu|Q1348656}}, {{jalink|Gifu Prefecture|Q131277}}. It is regarded as Kanda Shrine listed in the {{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932}} for {{jalink|Mino Province|Q864156}} Kamo District. The annual grand festival in late September includes ritual ceremonies, lion dance, and ceremonial sake distribution. Floats used to be pulled through the village, but this stopped in the 1950s due to depopulation. In {{jalink|2008|Q2004}}（{{jalink|Heisei|Q234510}} 20）, a new float was built and the procession revived. === Overview === It is said to have been founded in {{jalink|Yōrō|Q1373061}} 2 ({{jalink|718|Q23534}}). In the Engishiki period, "Kanda" is said to have been read as "Kamuda." It declined after the mid-Heian period, but was restored in Kakei 2 ({{jalink|1388|Q6365}}), and through {{jalink|Shinbutsu-shūgō|Q2279088}} was renamed '''Hakusan Myōdaigongen'''. It declined again in the Muromachi period, but was restored in Eiroku 1 ({{jalink|1558|Q6601}}). Buildings were rebuilt in Kyōhō 2 ({{jalink|1717|Q6895}}). In Meiji 1 ({{jalink|1868|Q7717}}), this area underwent thorough {{jalink|haibutsu kishaku|Q702298}} under the {{jalink|Naegi Domain|Q10863458}}, and Hakusan Myōdaigongen was renamed Kanda Shrine. In Meiji 3 ({{jalink|1870|Q7741}}), the {{jalink|Department of Divinities}} recognized it as the Shikinaisha Kanda Shrine. In Meiji 4 ({{jalink|1871|Q7746}}) it became a gōsha of Naegi Prefecture, and in Meiji 6 ({{jalink|1873|Q7757}}) a gōsha of Gifu Prefecture. On July 1, 1952 (Shōwa 27), it was designated by Gifu Prefectural Shrine Office as a shrine receiving regular offerings (Kinpei-sha designation No. 13){{sfn|木村|1997|p=121}}. === Enshrined deities === === Main deities === *{{jalink|Izanagi|Q813858|Izanagi-no-Mikoto}} *{{jalink|Izanami|Q682306|Izanami-no-Mikoto}} *{{jalink|Ōkuninushi|Q276944|Ōkuninushi-no-Mikoto}} *{{jalink|Sukunabikona|Q3275687|Sukunahikona-no-Mikoto}} *{{jalink|Kumano Gongen|Q11568925|Hayatama-no-o-no-Mikoto}} === Merged enshrined deities === *{{jalink|Sarutahiko|Q3090037|Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto}} *{{jalink|Ōyamatsumi|Q386563|Ōyamazumi-no-Mikoto}} *{{jalink|Mizuwake-no-Mikoto}} *{{jalink|Amaterasu||Amaterasu-Ōmikami}} *{{jalink|Kukurihime|Q11619891|Kukurihime-no-Mikoto}} *{{jalink|Emperor Nintoku|Q313119|Ōsazaki-no-Mikoto}} *{{jalink|Kagutsuchi|Q1640658|Homusubi-no-Mikoto}} *{{jalink|Homuda-wake-no-Mikoto}}（{{jalink|Hachiman deity|Q261637}}） *{{jalink|Susanoo|Q272993|Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} === Address === * Gifu Prefecture, Kamo District, Higashishirakawa Village, Kando 604 === Transportation === * {{jalink|Nōhi Bus|Q11566124|Nōhi Bus}} Shirakawa Line, get off at "{{jalink|Higashishirakawa Village Office|Q109368715}} Mae". ** From {{jalink|Shirakawa-guchi Station|Q5358687}} on the {{jalink|Takayama Main Line|Q1188357}}, bus bound for Daimyōjin. === References === * {{Cite book |和書 |editor=Teru Kimura |title=Major Shrine Tour in Gifu Prefecture |publisher={{jalink|Nagaranoten Jinja|Q3335060}} |date=1997 |ref={{SfnRef|木村|1997}}}} === External links === * [https://yaokami.jp/1212597/ Yaokami] === Related items === * {{jalink|Koshihara Shrine}}[http://www.gifu-jinjacho.jp/syosai.php?shrno=522&shrname=%E2%96%A0%E8%B6%8A%E5%8E%9F%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE%E2%96%A0 Shrine Office] * {{jalink|Goka Shrine}} === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} Torii and haiden | 位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|38|37|N|137|19|25|E|display=title,inline}} | 所在地 = {{jalink|Gifu Prefecture|Q131277}}{{jalink|Kamo District, Gifu|Q1348656}}{{jalink|Higashishirakawa|Q1202765}}{{jalink|Kando}}604 | 祭神= | 社格= {{jalink|Shikinai Shosha|Q134917287|Shikinaisha}}（small）・former {{jalink|Gōsha|Q134917316}}・{{jalink|Kinpei-sha|Q119929592}} | 別名 = {{jalink|Hakusan Myōdaigongen}}（{{jalink|Hakusan Myōri Gongen}}） | 創建= {{jalink|Yōrō|Q1373061}} 2 | 例祭= late September | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Gifu|label=Kanda Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Kanda Shrine'''（かんだじんじゃ）is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} in {{jalink|Kando}}, {{jalink|Higashishirakawa|Q1202765}}, {{jalink|Kamo District, Gifu|Q1348656}}, {{jalink|Gifu Prefecture|Q131277}}. It is regarded as Kanda Shrine listed in the {{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932}} for {{jalink|Mino Province|Q864156}} Kamo District. The annual grand festival in late September includes ritual ceremonies, lion dance, and ceremonial sake distribution. Floats used to be pulled through the village, but this stopped in the 1950s due to depopulation. In {{jalink|2008|Q2004}}（{{jalink|Heisei|Q234510}} 20）, a new float was built and the procession revived. === Overview === It is said to have been founded in {{jalink|Yōrō|Q1373061}} 2 ({{jalink|718|Q23534}}). In the Engishiki period, "Kanda" is said to have been read as "Kamuda." It declined after the mid-Heian period, but was restored in Kakei 2 ({{jalink|1388|Q6365}}), and through {{jalink|Shinbutsu-shūgō|Q2279088}} was renamed '''Hakusan Myōdaigongen'''. It declined again in the Muromachi period, but was restored in Eiroku 1 ({{jalink|1558|Q6601}}). Buildings were rebuilt in Kyōhō 2 ({{jalink|1717|Q6895}}). In Meiji 1 ({{jalink|1868|Q7717}}), this area underwent thorough {{jalink|haibutsu kishaku|Q702298}} under the {{jalink|Naegi Domain|Q10863458}}, and Hakusan Myōdaigongen was renamed Kanda Shrine. In Meiji 3 ({{jalink|1870|Q7741}}), the {{jalink|Department of Divinities}} recognized it as the Shikinaisha Kanda Shrine. In Meiji 4 ({{jalink|1871|Q7746}}) it became a gōsha of Naegi Prefecture, and in Meiji 6 ({{jalink|1873|Q7757}}) a gōsha of Gifu Prefecture. On July 1, 1952 (Shōwa 27), it was designated by Gifu Prefectural Shrine Office as a shrine receiving regular offerings (Kinpei-sha designation No. 13){{sfn|木村|1997|p=121}}. === Enshrined deities === === Main deities === *{{jalink|Izanagi|Q813858|Izanagi-no-Mikoto}} *{{jalink|Izanami|Q682306|Izanami-no-Mikoto}} *{{jalink|Ōkuninushi|Q276944|Ōkuninushi-no-Mikoto}} *{{jalink|Sukunabikona|Q3275687|Sukunahikona-no-Mikoto}} *{{jalink|Kumano Gongen|Q11568925|Hayatama-no-o-no-Mikoto}} === Merged enshrined deities === *{{jalink|Sarutahiko|Q3090037|Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto}} *{{jalink|Ōyamatsumi|Q386563|Ōyamazumi-no-Mikoto}} *{{jalink|Mizuwake-no-Mikoto}} *{{jalink|Amaterasu||Amaterasu-Ōmikami}} *{{jalink|Kukurihime|Q11619891|Kukurihime-no-Mikoto}} *{{jalink|Emperor Nintoku|Q313119|Ōsazaki-no-Mikoto}} *{{jalink|Kagutsuchi|Q1640658|Homusubi-no-Mikoto}} *{{jalink|Homuda-wake-no-Mikoto}}（{{jalink|Hachiman deity|Q261637}}） *{{jalink|Susanoo|Q272993|Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} === Address === * Gifu Prefecture, Kamo District, Higashishirakawa Village, Kando 604 === Transportation === * {{jalink|Nōhi Bus|Q11566124|Nōhi Bus}} Shirakawa Line, get off at "{{jalink|Higashishirakawa Village Office|Q109368715}} Mae". ** From {{jalink|Shirakawa-guchi Station|Q5358687}} on the {{jalink|Takayama Main Line|Q1188357}}, bus bound for Daimyōjin. === References === * {{Cite book |和書 |editor=Teru Kimura |title=Major Shrine Tour in Gifu Prefecture |publisher={{jalink|Nagaranoten Jinja|Q3335060}} |date=1997 |ref={{SfnRef|木村|1997}}}} === External links === * [https://yaokami.jp/1212597/ Yaokami] === Related items === * {{jalink|Koshihara Shrine}}[http://www.gifu-jinjacho.jp/syosai.php?shrno=522&shrname=%E2%96%A0%E8%B6%8A%E5%8E%9F%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE%E2%96%A0 Shrine Office] * {{jalink|Goka Shrine}} === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} Torii and haiden | 位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|38|37|N|137|19|25|E|display=title,inline}} | 所在地 = {{jalink|Gifu Prefecture|Q131277}}{{jalink|Kamo District, Gifu|Q1348656}}{{jalink|Higashishirakawa|Q1202765}}{{jalink|Kando}}604 | 祭神= | 社格= {{jalink|Shikinai Shosha|Q134917287|Shikinaisha}}（small）・former {{jalink|Gōsha|Q134917316}}・{{jalink|Kinpei-sha|Q119929592}} | 別名 = {{jalink|Hakusan Myōdaigongen}}（{{jalink|Hakusan Myōri Gongen}}） | 創建= {{jalink|Yōrō|Q1373061}} 2 | 例祭= late September | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Gifu|label=Kanda Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Kanda Shrine'''（かんだじんじゃ）is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} in {{jalink|Kando}}, {{jalink|Higashishirakawa|Q1202765}}, {{jalink|Kamo District, Gifu|Q1348656}}, {{jalink|Gifu Prefecture|Q131277}}. It is regarded as Kanda Shrine listed in the {{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932}} for {{jalink|Mino Province|Q864156}} Kamo District. The annual grand festival in late September includes ritual ceremonies, lion dance, and ceremonial sake distribution. Floats used to be pulled through the village, but this stopped in the 1950s due to depopulation. In {{jalink|2008|Q2004}}（{{jalink|Heisei|Q234510}} 20）, a new float was built and the procession revived. === Overview === It is said to have been founded in {{jalink|Yōrō|Q1373061}} 2 ({{jalink|718|Q23534}}). In the Engishiki period, "Kanda" is said to have been read as "Kamuda." It declined after the mid-Heian period, but was restored in Kakei 2 ({{jalink|1388|Q6365}}), and through {{jalink|Shinbutsu-shūgō|Q2279088}} was renamed '''Hakusan Myōdaigongen'''. It declined again in the Muromachi period, but was restored in Eiroku 1 ({{jalink|1558|Q6601}}). Buildings were rebuilt in Kyōhō 2 ({{jalink|1717|Q6895}}). In Meiji 1 ({{jalink|1868|Q7717}}), this area underwent thorough {{jalink|haibutsu kishaku|Q702298}} under the {{jalink|Naegi Domain|Q10863458}}, and Hakusan Myōdaigongen was renamed Kanda Shrine. In Meiji 3 ({{jalink|1870|Q7741}}), the {{jalink|Department of Divinities}} recognized it as the Shikinaisha Kanda Shrine. In Meiji 4 ({{jalink|1871|Q7746}}) it became a gōsha of Naegi Prefecture, and in Meiji 6 ({{jalink|1873|Q7757}}) a gōsha of Gifu Prefecture. On July 1, 1952 (Shōwa 27), it was designated by Gifu Prefectural Shrine Office as a shrine receiving regular offerings (Kinpei-sha designation No. 13){{sfn|木村|1997|p=121}}. === Enshrined deities === === Main deities === *{{jalink|Izanagi|Q813858|Izanagi-no-Mikoto}} *{{jalink|Izanami|Q682306|Izanami-no-Mikoto}} *{{jalink|Ōkuninushi|Q276944|Ōkuninushi-no-Mikoto}} *{{jalink|Sukunabikona|Q3275687|Sukunahikona-no-Mikoto}} *{{jalink|Kumano Gongen|Q11568925|Hayatama-no-o-no-Mikoto}} === Merged enshrined deities === *{{jalink|Sarutahiko|Q3090037|Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto}} *{{jalink|Ōyamatsumi|Q386563|Ōyamazumi-no-Mikoto}} *{{jalink|Mizuwake-no-Mikoto}} *{{jalink|Amaterasu||Amaterasu-Ōmikami}} *{{jalink|Kukurihime|Q11619891|Kukurihime-no-Mikoto}} *{{jalink|Emperor Nintoku|Q313119|Ōsazaki-no-Mikoto}} *{{jalink|Kagutsuchi|Q1640658|Homusubi-no-Mikoto}} *{{jalink|Homuda-wake-no-Mikoto}}（{{jalink|Hachiman deity|Q261637}}） *{{jalink|Susanoo|Q272993|Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} === Address === * Gifu Prefecture, Kamo District, Higashishirakawa Village, Kando 604 === Transportation === * {{jalink|Nōhi Bus|Q11566124|Nōhi Bus}} Shirakawa Line, get off at "{{jalink|Higashishirakawa Village Office|Q109368715}} Mae". ** From {{jalink|Shirakawa-guchi Station|Q5358687}} on the {{jalink|Takayama Main Line|Q1188357}}, bus bound for Daimyōjin. === References === * {{Cite book |和書 |editor=Teru Kimura |title=Major Shrine Tour in Gifu Prefecture |publisher={{jalink|Nagaranoten Jinja|Q3335060}} |date=1997 |ref={{SfnRef|木村|1997}}}} === External links === * [https://yaokami.jp/1212597/ Yaokami] === Related items === * {{jalink|Koshihara Shrine}}[http://www.gifu-jinjacho.jp/syosai.php?shrno=522&shrname=%E2%96%A0%E8%B6%8A%E5%8E%9F%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE%E2%96%A0 Shrine Office] * {{jalink|Goka Shrine}} === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} link|Q11590647}} == Japanese content == {{神社 | 名称 = Kanda Shrine | 画像 = [[File:Kanda jinja Higashishirakawa.jpg|250px]]
Torii and haiden | 位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|38|37|N|137|19|25|E|display=title,inline}} | 所在地 = {{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|WD=Q131277}}{{ill|lt=Kamo District, Gifu|Q1348656|WD=Q1348656}}{{ill|lt=Higashishirakawa|Q1202765|WD=Q1202765}}{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kando|UNKNOWN}}604 | 祭神= | 社格= {{ill|lt=Shikinai Shosha|Q134917287|WD=Q134917287|lt=Shikinaisha}}（small）・former {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}}・{{ill|lt=Kinpei-sha|Q119929592|WD=Q119929592}} | 別名 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Hakusan Myōdaigongen|UNKNOWN}}（{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Hakusan Myōri Gongen|UNKNOWN}}） | 創建= {{ill|lt=Yōrō|Q1373061|WD=Q1373061}} 2 | 例祭= late September | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Gifu|label=Kanda Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Kanda Shrine'''（かんだじんじゃ）is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kando|UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|lt=Higashishirakawa|Q1202765|WD=Q1202765}}, {{ill|lt=Kamo District, Gifu|Q1348656|WD=Q1348656}}, {{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|WD=Q131277}}. It is regarded as Kanda Shrine listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}} for {{ill|lt=Mino Province|Q864156|WD=Q864156}} Kamo District. The annual grand festival in late September includes ritual ceremonies, lion dance, and ceremonial sake distribution. Floats used to be pulled through the village, but this stopped in the 1950s due to depopulation. In {{ill|lt=2008|Q2004|WD=Q2004}}（{{ill|lt=Heisei|Q234510|WD=Q234510}} 20）, a new float was built and the procession revived. === Overview === It is said to have been founded in {{ill|lt=Yōrō|Q1373061|WD=Q1373061}} 2 ({{ill|lt=718|Q23534|WD=Q23534}}). In the Engishiki period, "Kanda" is said to have been read as "Kamuda." It declined after the mid-Heian period, but was restored in Kakei 2 ({{ill|lt=1388|Q6365|WD=Q6365}}), and through {{ill|lt=Shinbutsu-shūgō|Q2279088|WD=Q2279088}} was renamed '''Hakusan Myōdaigongen'''. It declined again in the Muromachi period, but was restored in Eiroku 1 ({{ill|lt=1558|Q6601|WD=Q6601}}). Buildings were rebuilt in Kyōhō 2 ({{ill|lt=1717|Q6895|WD=Q6895}}). In Meiji 1 ({{ill|lt=1868|Q7717|WD=Q7717}}), this area underwent thorough {{ill|lt=haibutsu kishaku|Q702298|WD=Q702298}} under the {{ill|lt=Naegi Domain|Q10863458|WD=Q10863458}}, and Hakusan Myōdaigongen was renamed Kanda Shrine. In Meiji 3 ({{ill|lt=1870|Q7741|WD=Q7741}}), the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Department of Divinities|UNKNOWN}} recognized it as the Shikinaisha Kanda Shrine. In Meiji 4 ({{ill|lt=1871|Q7746|WD=Q7746}}) it became a gōsha of Naegi Prefecture, and in Meiji 6 ({{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757}}) a gōsha of Gifu Prefecture. On July 1, 1952 (Shōwa 27), it was designated by Gifu Prefectural Shrine Office as a shrine receiving regular offerings (Kinpei-sha designation No. 13){{sfn|木村|1997|p=121}}. === Enshrined deities === === Main deities === *{{ill|lt=Izanagi|Q813858|WD=Q813858|lt=Izanagi-no-Mikoto}} *{{ill|lt=Izanami|Q682306|WD=Q682306|lt=Izanami-no-Mikoto}} Gongen|Q11568925|WD=Q11568925|lt=Hayatama-no-o-no-Mikoto}} === Merged enshrined deities === *{{ill|lt=Sarutahiko|Q3090037|WD=Q3090037|lt=Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto}} *{{ill|lt=Ōyamatsumi|Q386563|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamazumi-no-Mikoto}} *{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Mizuwake-no-Mikoto|UNKNOWN}} *{{ill|lt=Amaterasu||WD=|lt=Amaterasu-Ōmikami}} *{{ill|lt=Kagutsuchi|Q1640658|WD=Q1640658|lt=Homusubi-no-Mikoto}} *{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Homuda-wake-no-Mikoto|UNKNOWN}}（{{ill|lt=Hachiman deity|Q261637|WD=Q261637}}） *{{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} === Address === * Gifu Prefecture, Kamo District, Higashishirakawa Village, Kando 604 === Transportation === * {{ill|lt=Nōhi link|Q11590647}} == Japanese content == {{神社 | 名称 = Kanda Shrine | 画像 = [[File:Kanda jinja Higashishirakawa.jpg|250px]]
Torii and haiden | 位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|38|37|N|137|19|25|E|display=title,inline}} | 所在地 = {{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|WD=Q131277}}{{ill|lt=Kamo District, Gifu|Q1348656|WD=Q1348656}}{{ill|lt=Higashishirakawa|Q1202765|WD=Q1202765}}{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kando|1=UNKNOWN}}604 | 祭神= | 社格= {{ill|lt=Shikinai Shosha|Q134917287|WD=Q134917287|lt=Shikinaisha}}（small）・former {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}}・{{ill|lt=Kinpei-sha|Q119929592|WD=Q119929592}} | 別名 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Hakusan Myōdaigongen|1=UNKNOWN}}（{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Hakusan Myōri Gongen|1=UNKNOWN}}） | 創建= {{ill|lt=Yōrō|Q1373061|WD=Q1373061}} 2 | 例祭= late September | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Gifu|label=Kanda Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Kanda Shrine'''（かんだじんじゃ）is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kando|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|lt=Higashishirakawa|Q1202765|WD=Q1202765}}, {{ill|lt=Kamo District, Gifu|Q1348656|WD=Q1348656}}, {{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|WD=Q131277}}. It is regarded as Kanda Shrine listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}} for {{ill|lt=Mino Province|Q864156|WD=Q864156}} Kamo District. The annual grand festival in late September includes ritual ceremonies, lion dance, and ceremonial sake distribution. Floats used to be pulled through the village, but this stopped in the 1950s due to depopulation. In {{ill|lt=2008|Q2004|WD=Q2004}}（{{ill|lt=Heisei|Q234510|WD=Q234510}} 20）, a new float was built and the procession revived. === Overview === It is said to have been founded in {{ill|lt=Yōrō|Q1373061|WD=Q1373061}} 2 ({{ill|lt=718|Q23534|WD=Q23534}}). In the Engishiki period, "Kanda" is said to have been read as "Kamuda." It declined after the mid-Heian period, but was restored in Kakei 2 ({{ill|lt=1388|Q6365|WD=Q6365}}), and through {{ill|lt=Shinbutsu-shūgō|Q2279088|WD=Q2279088}} was renamed '''Hakusan Myōdaigongen'''. It declined again in the Muromachi period, but was restored in Eiroku 1 ({{ill|lt=1558|Q6601|WD=Q6601}}). Buildings were rebuilt in Kyōhō 2 ({{ill|lt=1717|Q6895|WD=Q6895}}). In Meiji 1 ({{ill|lt=1868|Q7717|WD=Q7717}}), this area underwent thorough {{ill|lt=haibutsu kishaku|Q702298|WD=Q702298}} under the {{ill|lt=Naegi Domain|Q10863458|WD=Q10863458}}, and Hakusan Myōdaigongen was renamed Kanda Shrine. In Meiji 3 ({{ill|lt=1870|Q7741|WD=Q7741}}), the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Department of Divinities|1=UNKNOWN}} recognized it as the Shikinaisha Kanda Shrine. In Meiji 4 ({{ill|lt=1871|Q7746|WD=Q7746}}) it became a gōsha of Naegi Prefecture, and in Meiji 6 ({{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757}}) a gōsha of Gifu Prefecture. On July 1, 1952 (Shōwa 27), it was designated by Gifu Prefectural Shrine Office as a shrine receiving regular offerings (Kinpei-sha designation No. 13){{sfn|木村|1997|p=121}}. === Enshrined deities === === Main deities === *{{ill|lt=Izanagi|Q813858|WD=Q813858|lt=Izanagi-no-Mikoto}} *{{ill|lt=Izanami|Q682306|WD=Q682306|lt=Izanami-no-Mikoto}} *{{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōkuninushi-no-Mikoto}} *{{ill|lt=Sukunabikona|Q3275687|WD=Q3275687|lt=Sukunahikona-no-Mikoto}} *{{ill|lt=Kumano Gongen|Q11568925|WD=Q11568925|lt=Hayatama-no-o-no-Mikoto}} === Merged enshrined deities === *{{ill|lt=Sarutahiko|Q3090037|WD=Q3090037|lt=Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto}} *{{ill|lt=Ōyamatsumi|Q386563|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamazumi-no-Mikoto}} *{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Mizuwake-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} *{{ill|lt=Amaterasu|UNKNOWN|WD=|lt=Amaterasu-Ōmikami}} *{{ill|lt=Kukurihime|Q11619891|WD=Q11619891|lt=Kukurihime-no-Mikoto}} *{{ill|lt=Emperor Nintoku|Q313119|WD=Q313119|lt=Ōsazaki-no-Mikoto}} *{{ill|lt=Kagutsuchi|Q1640658|WD=Q1640658|lt=Homusubi-no-Mikoto}} *{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Homuda-wake-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}（{{ill|lt=Hachiman deity|Q261637|WD=Q261637}}） *{{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} === Address === * Gifu Prefecture, Kamo District, Higashishirakawa Village, Kando 604 === Transportation === * {{ill|lt=Nōhi Bus|Q11566124|WD=Q11566124|lt=Nōhi Bus}} Shirakawa Line, get off at "{{ill|lt=Higashishirakawa Village Office|Q109368715|WD=Q109368715}} Mae". ** From {{ill|lt=Shirakawa-guchi link|Q11590647}} == Japanese content == {{神社 | 名称 = Kanda Shrine | 画像 = [[File:Kanda jinja Higashishirakawa.jpg|250px]]
Torii and haiden | 位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|38|37|N|137|19|25|E|display=title,inline}} | 所在地 = {{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|WD=Q131277}}{{ill|lt=Kamo District, Gifu|Q1348656|WD=Q1348656}}{{ill|lt=Higashishirakawa|Q1202765|WD=Q1202765}}{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kando|1=UNKNOWN}}604 | 祭神= | 社格= {{ill|lt=Shikinai Shosha|Q134917287|WD=Q134917287|lt=Shikinaisha}}（small）・former {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}}・{{ill|lt=Kinpei-sha|Q119929592|WD=Q119929592}} | 別名 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Hakusan Myōdaigongen|1=UNKNOWN}}（{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Hakusan Myōri Gongen|1=UNKNOWN}}） | 創建= {{ill|lt=Yōrō|Q1373061|WD=Q1373061}} 2 | 例祭= late September | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Gifu|label=Kanda Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Kanda Shrine'''（かんだじんじゃ）is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kando|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|lt=Higashishirakawa|Q1202765|WD=Q1202765}}, {{ill|lt=Kamo District, Gifu|Q1348656|WD=Q1348656}}, {{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|WD=Q131277}}. It is regarded as Kanda Shrine listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}} for {{ill|lt=Mino Province|Q864156|WD=Q864156}} Kamo District. The annual grand festival in late September includes ritual ceremonies, lion dance, and ceremonial sake distribution. Floats used to be pulled through the village, but this stopped in the 1950s due to depopulation. In {{ill|lt=2008|Q2004|WD=Q2004}}（{{ill|lt=Heisei|Q234510|WD=Q234510}} 20）, a new float was built and the procession revived. === Overview === It is said to have been founded in {{ill|lt=Yōrō|Q1373061|WD=Q1373061}} 2 ({{ill|lt=718|Q23534|WD=Q23534}}). In the Engishiki period, "Kanda" is said to have been read as "Kamuda." It declined after the mid-Heian period, but was restored in Kakei 2 ({{ill|lt=1388|Q6365|WD=Q6365}}), and through {{ill|lt=Shinbutsu-shūgō|Q2279088|WD=Q2279088}} was renamed '''Hakusan Myōdaigongen'''. It declined again in the Muromachi period, but was restored in Eiroku 1 ({{ill|lt=1558|Q6601|WD=Q6601}}). Buildings were rebuilt in Kyōhō 2 ({{ill|lt=1717|Q6895|WD=Q6895}}). In Meiji 1 ({{ill|lt=1868|Q7717|WD=Q7717}}), this area underwent thorough {{ill|lt=haibutsu kishaku|Q702298|WD=Q702298}} under the {{ill|lt=Naegi Domain|Q10863458|WD=Q10863458}}, and Hakusan Myōdaigongen was renamed Kanda Shrine. In Meiji 3 ({{ill|lt=1870|Q7741|WD=Q7741}}), the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Department of Divinities|1=UNKNOWN}} recognized it as the Shikinaisha Kanda Shrine. In Meiji 4 ({{ill|lt=1871|Q7746|WD=Q7746}}) it became a gōsha of Naegi Prefecture, and in Meiji 6 ({{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757}}) a gōsha of Gifu Prefecture. On July 1, 1952 (Shōwa 27), it was designated by Gifu Prefectural Shrine Office as a shrine receiving regular offerings (Kinpei-sha designation No. 13){{sfn|木村|1997|p=121}}. === Enshrined deities === === Main deities === *{{ill|lt=Izanagi|Q813858|WD=Q813858|lt=Izanagi-no-Mikoto}} *{{ill|lt=Izanami|Q682306|WD=Q682306|lt=Izanami-no-Mikoto}} Gongen|Q11568925|WD=Q11568925|lt=Hayatama-no-o-no-Mikoto}} === Merged enshrined deities === *{{ill|lt=Sarutahiko|Q3090037|WD=Q3090037|lt=Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto}} *{{ill|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi|Q386563|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamazumi-no-Mikoto}} *{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Mizuwake-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} *{{ill|lt=Amaterasu|UNKNOWN|lt=Amaterasu-Ōmikami}} *{{ill|lt=Kukurihime|Q11619891|WD=Q11619891|lt=Kukurihime-no-Mikoto}} *{{ill|lt=Emperor Nintoku|Q313119|WD=Q313119|lt=Ōsazaki-no-Mikoto}} *{{ill|lt=Kagutsuchi|Q1640658|WD=Q1640658|lt=Homusubi-no-Mikoto}} *{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Homuda-wake-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}（{{ill|lt=Hachiman deity|Q261637|WD=Q261637}}） *{{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} === Address === * Gifu Prefecture, Kamo District, Higashishirakawa Village, Kando 604 === Transportation === * {{ill|lt=Nōhi Bus|Q11566124|WD=Q11566124|lt=Nōhi Bus}} Shirakawa Line, get off at "{{ill|lt=Higashishirakawa Village Office|Q109368715|WD=Q109368715}} Mae". ** From {{ill|lt=Shirakawa-guchi Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 5200005006637 == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '岐阜県加茂郡東白川村神土604', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:神田神社 (東白川村)]] {{wikidata link|Q11590647}} == Japanese content == {{神社 | 名称 = Kanda Shrine | 画像 = [[File:Kanda jinja Higashishirakawa.jpg|250px]]
Torii and haiden | 位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|38|37|N|137|19|25|E|display=title,inline}} | 所在地 = {{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|WD=Q131277|1=Gifu Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Kamo District, Gifu|Q1348656|WD=Q1348656|1=Kamo District, Gifu}}{{ill|lt=Higashishirakawa|Q1202765|WD=Q1202765|1=Higashishirakawa, Gifu}}{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kando|1=UNKNOWN}}604 | 祭神= | 社格= {{ill|lt=Shikinai Shosha|Q134917287|WD=Q134917287|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinai Shosha}}（small）・former {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}・{{ill|lt=Kinpei-sha|Q119929592|WD=Q119929592|1=Kinpei-sha}} | 別名 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Hakusan Myōdaigongen|1=UNKNOWN}}（{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Hakusan Myōri Gongen|1=UNKNOWN}}） | 創建= {{ill|lt=Yōrō|Q1373061|WD=Q1373061|1=Yōrō}} 2 | 例祭= late September | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Gifu|label=Kanda Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Kanda Shrine'''（かんだじんじゃ）is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kando|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|lt=Higashishirakawa|Q1202765|WD=Q1202765|1=Higashishirakawa, Gifu}}, {{ill|lt=Kamo District, Gifu|Q1348656|WD=Q1348656|1=Kamo District, Gifu}}, {{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|WD=Q131277|1=Gifu Prefecture}}. It is regarded as Kanda Shrine listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} for {{ill|lt=Mino Province|Q864156|WD=Q864156|1=Mino Province}} Kamo District. The annual grand festival in late September includes ritual ceremonies, lion dance, and ceremonial sake distribution. Floats used to be pulled through the village, but this stopped in the 1950s due to depopulation. In {{ill|lt=2008|Q2004|WD=Q2004|1=2008}}（{{ill|lt=Heisei|Q234510|WD=Q234510|1=Heisei era}} 20）, a new float was built and the procession revived. === Overview === It is said to have been founded in {{ill|lt=Yōrō|Q1373061|WD=Q1373061|1=Yōrō}} 2 ({{ill|lt=718|Q23534|WD=Q23534|1=718}}). In the Engishiki period, "Kanda" is said to have been read as "Kamuda." It declined after the mid-Heian period, but was restored in Kakei 2 ({{ill|lt=1388|Q6365|WD=Q6365|1=1388}}), and through {{ill|lt=Shinbutsu-shūgō|Q2279088|WD=Q2279088|1=Shinbutsu-shūgō}} was renamed '''Hakusan Myōdaigongen'''. It declined again in the Muromachi period, but was restored in Eiroku 1 ({{ill|lt=1558|Q6601|WD=Q6601|1=1558}}). Buildings were rebuilt in Kyōhō 2 ({{ill|lt=1717|Q6895|WD=Q6895|1=1717}}). In Meiji 1 ({{ill|lt=1868|Q7717|WD=Q7717|1=1868}}), this area underwent thorough {{ill|lt=haibutsu kishaku|Q702298|WD=Q702298|1=Haibutsu kishaku}} under the {{ill|lt=Naegi Domain|Q10863458|WD=Q10863458|1=Naegi Domain}}, and Hakusan Myōdaigongen was renamed Kanda Shrine. In Meiji 3 ({{ill|lt=1870|Q7741|WD=Q7741|1=1870}}), the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Department of Divinities|1=UNKNOWN}} recognized it as the Shikinaisha Kanda Shrine. In Meiji 4 ({{ill|lt=1871|Q7746|WD=Q7746|1=1871}}) it became a gōsha of Naegi Prefecture, and in Meiji 6 ({{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757|1=1873}}) a gōsha of Gifu Prefecture. On July 1, 1952 (Shōwa 27), it was designated by Gifu Prefectural Shrine Office as a shrine receiving regular offerings (Kinpei-sha designation No. 13){{sfn|木村|1997|p=121}}. === Enshrined deities === === Main deities === *{{ill|lt=Izanagi|Q813858|WD=Q813858|lt=Izanagi-no-Mikoto|1=Izanagi}} *{{ill|lt=Izanami|Q682306|WD=Q682306|lt=Izanami-no-Mikoto|1=Izanami}} *{{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōkuninushi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōkuninushi}} *{{ill|lt=Sukunabikona|Q3275687|WD=Q3275687|lt=Sukunahikona-no-Mikoto|1=Sukunabikona}} *{{ill|lt=Kumano Gongen|Q11568925|WD=Q11568925|lt=Hayatama-no-o-no-Mikoto|1=Kumano Gongen}} === Merged enshrined deities === *{{ill|lt=Sarutahiko|Q3090037|WD=Q3090037|lt=Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto|1=Sarutahiko Ōkami}} *{{ill|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi|Q386563|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamazumi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōyamatsumi}} *{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Mizuwake-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} *{{ill|lt=Amaterasu|UNKNOWN|lt=Amaterasu-Ōmikami}} *{{ill|lt=Kukurihime|Q11619891|WD=Q11619891|lt=Kukurihime-no-Mikoto|1=Kukurihime}} *{{ill|lt=Emperor Nintoku|Q313119|WD=Q313119|lt=Ōsazaki-no-Mikoto|1=Emperor Nintoku}} *{{ill|lt=Kagutsuchi|Q1640658|WD=Q1640658|lt=Homusubi-no-Mikoto|1=Kagu-tsuchi}} *{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Homuda-wake-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}（{{ill|lt=Hachiman deity|Q261637|WD=Q261637|1=Hachiman}}） *{{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} === Address === * Gifu Prefecture, Kamo District, Higashishirakawa Village, Kando 604 === Transportation === * {{ill|lt=Nōhi Bus|Q11566124|WD=Q11566124|lt=Nōhi Bus|1=Nohi Noriai Jidosha}} Shirakawa Line, get off at "{{ill|lt=Higashishirakawa Village Office|Q109368715|WD=Q109368715|1=Higashishirakawa Village Office}} Mae". ** From {{ill|lt=Shirakawa-guchi Station|Q5358687|WD=Q5358687|1=Shirakawaguchi Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Takayama Main Line|Q1188357|WD=Q1188357|1=Takayama Main Line}}, bus bound for Daimyōjin. === References === * {{Cite book |和書 |editor=Teru Kimura |title=Major Shrine Tour in Gifu Prefecture |publisher={{ill|lt=Nagaranoten Jinja|Q3335060|WD=Q3335060|1=Nagara Tenjin Shrine}} |date=1997 |ref={{SfnRef|木村|1997}}}} === External links === * [https://yaokami.jp/1212597/ Yaokami] === Related items === * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Koshihara Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}}[http://www.gifu-jinjacho.jp/syosai.php?shrno=522&shrname=%E2%96%A0%E8%B6%8A%E5%8E%9F%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE%E2%96%A0 Shrine Office] * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Goka Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} === Notes Floats used to be pulled through the village, but this stopped in the 1950s due to depopulation. In {{ill|lt=2008|Q2004|WD=Q2004|1=2008}}（{{ill|lt=Heisei|Q234510|WD=Q234510|1=Heisei era}} 20）, a new float was built and the procession revived. === Overview === It is said to have been founded in {{ill|lt=Yōrō|Q1373061|WD=Q1373061|1=Yōrō}} 2 ({{ill|lt=718|Q23534|WD=Q23534|1=718}}). In the Engishiki period, "Kanda" is said to have been read as "Kamuda." It declined after the mid-Heian period, but was restored in Kakei 2 ({{ill|lt=1388|Q6365|WD=Q6365|1=1388}}), and through {{ill|lt=Shinbutsu-shūgō|Q2279088|WD=Q2279088|1=Shinbutsu-shūgō}} was renamed '''Hakusan Myōdaigongen'''. It declined again in the Muromachi period, but was restored in Eiroku 1 ({{ill|lt=1558|Q6601|WD=Q6601|1=1558}}). Buildings were rebuilt in Kyōhō 2 ({{ill|lt=1717|Q6895|WD=Q6895|1=1717}}). In Meiji 1 ({{ill|lt=1868|Q7717|WD=Q7717|1=1868}}), this area underwent thorough {{ill|lt=haibutsu kishaku|Q702298|WD=Q702298|1=Haibutsu kishaku}} under the {{ill|lt=Naegi Domain|Q10863458|WD=Q10863458|1=Naegi Domain}}, and Hakusan Myōdaigongen was renamed Kanda Shrine. In Meiji 3 ({{ill|lt=1870|Q7741|WD=Q7741|1=1870}}), the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Department of Divinities|1=UNKNOWN}} recognized it as the Shikinaisha Kanda Shrine. In Meiji 4 ({{ill|lt=1871|Q7746|WD=Q7746|1=1871}}) it became a gōsha of Naegi Prefecture, and in Meiji 6 ({{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757|1=1873}}) a gōsha of Gifu Prefecture. On July 1, 1952 (Shōwa 27), it was designated by Gifu Prefectural Shrine Office as a shrine receiving regular offerings (Kinpei-sha designation No. 13){{sfn|木村|1997|p=121}}. === Enshrined deities === === Main deities === *{{ill|lt=Izanagi|Q813858|WD=Q813858|lt=Izanagi-no-Mikoto|1=Izanagi}} *{{ill|lt=Izanami|Q682306|WD=Q682306|lt=Izanami-no-Mikoto|1=Izanami}} Gongen|Q11568925|WD=Q11568925|lt=Hayatama-no-o-no-Mikoto|1=Kumano Gongen}} === Merged enshrined deities === *{{ill|lt=Sarutahiko|Q3090037|WD=Q3090037|lt=Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto|1=Sarutahiko Ōkami}} *{{ill|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi|Q386563|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamazumi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōyamatsumi}} *{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Mizuwake-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} *{{ill|lt=Amaterasu|UNKNOWN|lt=Amaterasu-Ōmikami}} *{{ill|lt=Kagutsuchi|Q1640658|WD=Q1640658|lt=Homusubi-no-Mikoto|1=Kagu-tsuchi}} *{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Homuda-wake-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}（{{ill|lt=Hachiman deity|Q261637|WD=Q261637|1=Hachiman}}） It is regarded as Kanda Shrine listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} for {{ill|lt=Mino Province|Q864156|WD=Q864156|1=Mino Province}} Kamo District. The annual grand festival in late September includes ritual ceremonies, lion dance, and ceremonial sake distribution. Floats used to be pulled through the village, but this stopped in the 1950s due to depopulation. In {{ill|lt=2008|Q2004|WD=Q2004|1=2008}}（{{ill|lt=Heisei|Q234510|WD=Q234510|1=Heisei era}} 20）, a new float was built and the procession revived. === Overview === It is said to have been founded in {{ill|lt=Yōrō|Q1373061|WD=Q1373061|1=Yōrō}} 2 ({{ill|lt=718|Q23534|WD=Q23534|1=718}}). It declined again in the Muromachi period, but was restored in Eiroku 1 ({{ill|lt=1558|Q6601|WD=Q6601|1=1558}}). Buildings were rebuilt in Kyōhō 2 ({{ill|lt=1717|Q6895|WD=Q6895|1=1717}}). In Meiji 1 ({{ill|lt=1868|Q7717|WD=Q7717|1=1868}}), this area underwent thorough {{ill|lt=haibutsu kishaku|Q702298|WD=Q702298|1=Haibutsu kishaku}} under the {{ill|lt=Naegi Domain|Q10863458|WD=Q10863458|1=Naegi Domain}}, and Hakusan Myōdaigongen was renamed Kanda Shrine. On July 1, 1952 (Shōwa 27), it was designated by Gifu Prefectural Shrine Office as a shrine receiving regular offerings (Kinpei-sha designation No. 13){{sfn|木村|1997|p=121}}. === Enshrined deities === Kanda Shrine | 画像 = [[File:Kanda jinja Higashishirakawa.jpg|250px]]
Torii and haiden | 位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|38|37|N|137|19|25|E|display=title,inline}} | 所在地 = {{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|WD=Q131277|1=Gifu Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Kamo District, Gifu|Q1348656|WD=Q1348656|1=Kamo District, Gifu}}{{ill|lt=Higashishirakawa|Q1202765|WD=Q1202765|1=Higashishirakawa, Gifu}}{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kando|1=UNKNOWN}}604 | 祭神= | 社格= {{ill|lt=Shikinai Shosha|Q134917287|WD=Q134917287|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinai Shosha}}（small）・former {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}・{{ill|lt=Kinpei-sha|Q119929592|WD=Q119929592|1=Kinpei-sha}} | 別名 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Hakusan Myōdaigongen|1=UNKNOWN}}（{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Hakusan Myōri Gongen|1=UNKNOWN}}） | 創建= {{ill|lt=Yōrō|Q1373061|WD=Q1373061|1=Yōrō}} 2 | 例祭= late September | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Gifu|label=Kanda Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Kanda Shrine'''（かんだじんじゃ）is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kando|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|lt=Higashishirakawa|Q1202765|WD=Q1202765|1=Higashishirakawa, Gifu}}, {{ill|lt=Kamo District, Gifu|Q1348656|WD=Q1348656|1=Kamo District, Gifu}}, {{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|WD=Q131277|1=Gifu Prefecture}}. It is regarded as Kanda Shrine listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} for {{ill|lt=Mino Province|Q864156|WD=Q864156|1=Mino Province}} Kamo District. The annual grand festival in late September includes ritual ceremonies, lion dance, and ceremonial sake distribution. Floats used to be pulled through the village, but this stopped in the 1950s due to depopulation. In {{ill|lt=2008|Q2004|WD=Q2004|1=2008}}（{{ill|lt=Heisei|Q234510|WD=Q234510|1=Heisei era}} 20）, a new float was built and the procession revived. === Overview === It is said to have been founded in {{ill|lt=Yōrō|Q1373061|WD=Q1373061|1=Yōrō}} 2 ({{ill|lt=718|Q23534|WD=Q23534|1=718}}). In the Engishiki period, "Kanda" is said to have been read as "Kamuda." It declined after the mid-Heian period, but was restored in Kakei 2 ({{ill|lt=1388|Q6365|WD=Q6365|1=1388}}), and through {{ill|lt=Shinbutsu-shūgō|Q2279088|WD=Q2279088|1=Shinbutsu-shūgō}} was renamed '''Hakusan Myōdaigongen'''. It declined again in the Muromachi period, but was restored in Eiroku 1 ({{ill|lt=1558|Q6601|WD=Q6601|1=1558}}). Buildings were rebuilt in Kyōhō 2 ({{ill|lt=1717|Q6895|WD=Q6895|1=1717}}). In Meiji 1 ({{ill|lt=1868|Q7717|WD=Q7717|1=1868}}), this area underwent thorough {{ill|lt=haibutsu kishaku|Q702298|WD=Q702298|1=Haibutsu kishaku}} under the {{ill|lt=Naegi Domain|Q10863458|WD=Q10863458|1=Naegi Domain}}, and Hakusan Myōdaigongen was renamed Kanda Shrine. In Meiji 3 ({{ill|lt=1870|Q7741|WD=Q7741|1=1870}}), the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Department of Divinities|1=UNKNOWN}} recognized it as the Shikinaisha Kanda Shrine. In Meiji 4 ({{ill|lt=1871|Q7746|WD=Q7746|1=1871}}) it became a gōsha of Naegi Prefecture, and in Meiji 6 ({{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757|1=1873}}) a gōsha of Gifu Prefecture. On July 1, 1952 (Shōwa 27), it was designated by Gifu Prefectural Shrine Office as a shrine receiving regular offerings (Kinpei-sha designation No. 13){{sfn|木村|1997|p=121}}. === Enshrined deities === === Main deities === *{{ill|lt=Izanagi|Q813858|WD=Q813858|lt=Izanagi-no-Mikoto|1=Izanagi}} *{{ill|lt=Izanami|Q682306|WD=Q682306|lt=Izanami-no-Mikoto|1=Izanami}} Gongen|Q11568925|WD=Q11568925|lt=Hayatama-no-o-no-Mikoto|1=Kumano Gongen}} === Merged enshrined deities === *{{ill|lt=Sarutahiko|Q3090037|WD=Q3090037|lt=Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto|1=Sarutahiko Ōkami}} *{{ill|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi|Q386563|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamazumi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōyamatsumi}} *{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Mizuwake-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} *{{ill|lt=Amaterasu|UNKNOWN|lt=Amaterasu-Ōmikami}} Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 5200005006637 == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '岐阜県加茂郡東白川村神土604', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:神田神社 (東白川村)]] {{wikidata link|Q11590647}} == Japanese content == {{神社 | 名称 = Kanda Shrine | 画像 = [[File:Kanda jinja Higashishirakawa.jpg|250px]]
Torii and haiden | 位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|38|37|N|137|19|25|E|display=title,inline}} | 所在地 = {{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|WD=Q131277|1=Gifu Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Kamo District, Gifu|Q1348656|WD=Q1348656|1=Kamo District, Gifu}}{{ill|lt=Higashishirakawa|Q1202765|WD=Q1202765|1=Higashishirakawa, Gifu}}{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kando|1=UNKNOWN}}604 | 祭神= | 社格= {{ill|lt=Shikinai Shosha|Q134917287|WD=Q134917287|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinai Shosha}}（small）・former {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}・{{ill|lt=Kinpei-sha|Q119929592|WD=Q119929592|1=Kinpei-sha}} | 別名 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Hakusan Myōdaigongen|1=UNKNOWN}}（{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Hakusan Myōri Gongen|1=UNKNOWN}}） | 創建= {{ill|lt=Yōrō|Q1373061|WD=Q1373061|1=Yōrō}} 2 | 例祭= late September | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Gifu|label=Kanda Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Kanda Shrine'''（かんだじんじゃ）is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kando|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|lt=Higashishirakawa|Q1202765|WD=Q1202765|1=Higashishirakawa, Gifu}}, {{ill|lt=Kamo District, Gifu|Q1348656|WD=Q1348656|1=Kamo District, Gifu}}, {{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|WD=Q131277|1=Gifu Prefecture}}. It is regarded as Kanda Shrine listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} for {{ill|lt=Mino Province|Q864156|WD=Q864156|1=Mino Province}} Kamo District. The annual grand festival in late September includes ritual ceremonies, lion dance, and ceremonial sake distribution. Floats used to be pulled through the village, but this stopped in the 1950s due to depopulation. In {{ill|lt=2008|Q2004|WD=Q2004|1=2008}}（{{ill|lt=Heisei|Q234510|WD=Q234510|1=Heisei era}} 20）, a new float was built and the procession revived. === Overview === It is said to have been founded in {{ill|lt=Yōrō|Q1373061|WD=Q1373061|1=Yōrō}} 2 ({{ill|lt=718|Q23534|WD=Q23534|1=718}}). In the Engishiki period, "Kanda" is said to have been read as "Kamuda." It declined after the mid-Heian period, but was restored in Kakei 2 ({{ill|lt=1388|Q6365|WD=Q6365|1=1388}}), and through {{ill|lt=Shinbutsu-shūgō|Q2279088|WD=Q2279088|1=Shinbutsu-shūgō}} was renamed '''Hakusan Myōdaigongen'''. It declined again in the Muromachi period, but was restored in Eiroku 1 ({{ill|lt=1558|Q6601|WD=Q6601|1=1558}}). Buildings were rebuilt in Kyōhō 2 ({{ill|lt=1717|Q6895|WD=Q6895|1=1717}}). In Meiji 1 ({{ill|lt=1868|Q7717|WD=Q7717|1=1868}}), this area underwent thorough {{ill|lt=haibutsu kishaku|Q702298|WD=Q702298|1=Haibutsu kishaku}} under the {{ill|lt=Naegi Domain|Q10863458|WD=Q10863458|1=Naegi Domain}}, and Hakusan Myōdaigongen was renamed Kanda Shrine. In Meiji 3 ({{ill|lt=1870|Q7741|WD=Q7741|1=1870}}), the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Department of Divinities|1=UNKNOWN}} recognized it as the Shikinaisha Kanda Shrine. In Meiji 4 ({{ill|lt=1871|Q7746|WD=Q7746|1=1871}}) it became a gōsha of Naegi Prefecture, and in Meiji 6 ({{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757|1=1873}}) a gōsha of Gifu Prefecture. On July 1, 1952 (Shōwa 27), it was designated by Gifu Prefectural Shrine Office as a shrine receiving regular offerings (Kinpei-sha designation No. 13){{sfn|木村|1997|p=121}}. === Enshrined deities === === Main deities === *{{ill|lt=Izanagi|Q813858|WD=Q813858|lt=Izanagi-no-Mikoto|1=Izanagi}} *{{ill|lt=Izanami|Q682306|WD=Q682306|lt=Izanami-no-Mikoto|1=Izanami}} *{{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōkuninushi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōkuninushi}} *{{ill|lt=Sukunabikona|Q3275687|WD=Q3275687|lt=Sukunahikona-no-Mikoto|1=Sukunabikona}} *{{ill|lt=Kumano Gongen|Q11568925|WD=Q11568925|lt=Hayatama-no-o-no-Mikoto|1=Kumano Gongen}} === Merged enshrined deities === *{{ill|lt=Sarutahiko|Q3090037|WD=Q3090037|lt=Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto|1=Sarutahiko Ōkami}} *{{ill|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi|Q386563|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamazumi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōyamatsumi}} *{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Mizuwake-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} *{{ill|lt=Amaterasu|UNKNOWN|lt=Amaterasu-Ōmikami}} *{{ill|lt=Kukurihime|Q11619891|WD=Q11619891|lt=Kukurihime-no-Mikoto|1=Kukurihime}} *{{ill|lt=Emperor Nintoku|Q313119|WD=Q313119|lt=Ōsazaki-no-Mikoto|1=Emperor Nintoku}} *{{ill|lt=Kagutsuchi|Q1640658|WD=Q1640658|lt=Homusubi-no-Mikoto|1=Kagu-tsuchi}} *{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Homuda-wake-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}（{{ill|lt=Hachiman deity|Q261637|WD=Q261637|1=Hachiman}}） *{{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} === Address === * Gifu Prefecture, Kamo District, Higashishirakawa Village, Kando 604 === Transportation === * {{ill|lt=Nōhi Bus|Q11566124|WD=Q11566124|lt=Nōhi Bus|1=Nohi Noriai Jidosha}} Shirakawa Line, get off at "{{ill|lt=Higashishirakawa Village Office|Q109368715|WD=Q109368715|1=Higashishirakawa Village Office}} Mae". ** From {{ill|lt=Shirakawa-guchi Station|Q5358687|WD=Q5358687|1=Shirakawaguchi Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Takayama Main Line|Q1188357|WD=Q1188357|1=Takayama Main Line}}, bus bound for Daimyōjin. === References === * {{Cite book |和書 |editor=Teru Kimura |title=Major Shrine Tour in Gifu Prefecture |publisher={{ill|lt=Nagaranoten Jinja|Q3335060|WD=Q3335060|1=Nagara Tenjin Shrine}} In {{ill|lt=2008|Q2004|WD=Q2004|1=2008}}（{{ill|lt=Heisei|Q234510|WD=Q234510|1=Heisei era}} 20）, a new float was built and the procession revived. === Overview === It is said to have been founded in {{ill|lt=Yōrō|Q1373061|WD=Q1373061|1=Yōrō}} 2 ({{ill|lt=718|Q23534|WD=Q23534|1=718}}). In the Engishiki period, "Kanda" is said to have been read as "Kamuda." It declined after the mid-Heian period, but was restored in Kakei 2 ({{ill|lt=1388|Q6365|WD=Q6365|1=1388}}), and through {{ill|lt=Shinbutsu-shūgō|Q2279088|WD=Q2279088|1=Shinbutsu-shūgō}} was renamed '''Hakusan Myōdaigongen'''. It declined again in the Muromachi period, but was restored in Eiroku 1 ({{ill|lt=1558|Q6601|WD=Q6601|1=1558}}). Buildings were rebuilt in Kyōhō 2 ({{ill|lt=1717|Q6895|WD=Q6895|1=1717}}). In Meiji 1 ({{ill|lt=1868|Q7717|WD=Q7717|1=1868}}), this area underwent thorough {{ill|lt=haibutsu kishaku|Q702298|WD=Q702298|1=Haibutsu kishaku}} under the {{ill|lt=Naegi Domain|Q10863458|WD=Q10863458|1=Naegi Domain}}, and Hakusan Myōdaigongen was renamed Kanda Shrine. In Meiji 3 ({{ill|lt=1870|Q7741|WD=Q7741|1=1870}}), the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Department of Divinities|1=UNKNOWN}} recognized it as the Shikinaisha Kanda Shrine. In Meiji 4 ({{ill|lt=1871|Q7746|WD=Q7746|1=1871}}) it became a gōsha of Naegi Prefecture, and in Meiji 6 ({{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757|1=1873}}) a gōsha of Gifu Prefecture. On July 1, 1952 (Shōwa 27), it was designated by Gifu Prefectural Shrine Office as a shrine receiving regular offerings (Kinpei-sha designation No. 13){{sfn|木村|1997|p=121}}. === Enshrined deities === === Main deities === *{{ill|lt=Izanagi|Q813858|WD=Q813858|lt=Izanagi-no-Mikoto|1=Izanagi}} *{{ill|lt=Izanami|Q682306|WD=Q682306|lt=Izanami-no-Mikoto|1=Izanami}} Kanda Shrine | 画像 = [[File:Kanda jinja Higashishirakawa.jpg|250px]]
Torii and haiden | 位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|38|37|N|137|19|25|E|display=title,inline}} | 所在地 = {{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|WD=Q131277|1=Gifu Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Kamo District, Gifu|Q1348656|WD=Q1348656|1=Kamo District, Gifu}}{{ill|lt=Higashishirakawa|Q1202765|WD=Q1202765|1=Higashishirakawa, Gifu}}{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kando|1=UNKNOWN}}604 | 祭神= | 社格= {{ill|lt=Shikinai Shosha|Q134917287|WD=Q134917287|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinai Shosha}}（small）・former {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}・{{ill|lt=Kinpei-sha|Q119929592|WD=Q119929592|1=Kinpei-sha}} | 別名 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Hakusan Myōdaigongen|1=UNKNOWN}}（{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Hakusan Myōri Gongen|1=UNKNOWN}}） | 創建= {{ill|lt=Yōrō|Q1373061|WD=Q1373061|1=Yōrō}} 2 | 例祭= late September | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Gifu|label=Kanda Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Kanda Shrine'''（かんだじんじゃ）is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kando|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|lt=Higashishirakawa|Q1202765|WD=Q1202765|1=Higashishirakawa, Gifu}}, {{ill|lt=Kamo District, Gifu|Q1348656|WD=Q1348656|1=Kamo District, Gifu}}, {{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|WD=Q131277|1=Gifu Prefecture}}. It is regarded as Kanda Shrine listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} for {{ill|lt=Mino Province|Q864156|WD=Q864156|1=Mino Province}} Kamo District. The annual grand festival in late September includes ritual ceremonies, lion dance, and ceremonial sake distribution. Floats used to be pulled through the village, but this stopped in the 1950s due to depopulation. In {{ill|lt=2008|Q2004|WD=Q2004|1=2008}}（{{ill|lt=Heisei|Q234510|WD=Q234510|1=Heisei era}} 20）, a new float was built and the procession revived. === Overview === It is said to have been founded in {{ill|lt=Yōrō|Q1373061|WD=Q1373061|1=Yōrō}} 2 ({{ill|lt=718|Q23534|WD=Q23534|1=718}}). In the Engishiki period, "Kanda" is said to have been read as "Kamuda." It declined after the mid-Heian period, but was restored in Kakei 2 ({{ill|lt=1388|Q6365|WD=Q6365|1=1388}}), and through {{ill|lt=Shinbutsu-shūgō|Q2279088|WD=Q2279088|1=Shinbutsu-shūgō}} was renamed '''Hakusan Myōdaigongen'''. It declined again in the Muromachi period, but was restored in Eiroku 1 ({{ill|lt=1558|Q6601|WD=Q6601|1=1558}}). Buildings were rebuilt in Kyōhō 2 ({{ill|lt=1717|Q6895|WD=Q6895|1=1717}}). In Meiji 1 ({{ill|lt=1868|Q7717|WD=Q7717|1=1868}}), this area underwent thorough {{ill|lt=haibutsu kishaku|Q702298|WD=Q702298|1=Haibutsu kishaku}} under the {{ill|lt=Naegi Domain|Q10863458|WD=Q10863458|1=Naegi Domain}}, and Hakusan Myōdaigongen was renamed Kanda Shrine. In Meiji 3 ({{ill|lt=1870|Q7741|WD=Q7741|1=1870}}), the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Department of Divinities|1=UNKNOWN}} recognized it as the Shikinaisha Kanda Shrine. In Meiji 4 ({{ill|lt=1871|Q7746|WD=Q7746|1=1871}}) it became a gōsha of Naegi Prefecture, and in Meiji 6 ({{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757|1=1873}}) a gōsha of Gifu Prefecture. On July 1, 1952 (Shōwa 27), it was designated by Gifu Prefectural Shrine Office as a shrine receiving regular offerings (Kinpei-sha designation No. 13){{sfn|木村|1997|p=121}}. === Enshrined deities === === Main deities === *{{ill|lt=Izanagi|Q813858|WD=Q813858|lt=Izanagi-no-Mikoto|1=Izanagi}} *{{ill|lt=Izanami|Q682306|WD=Q682306|lt=Izanami-no-Mikoto|1=Izanami}} *{{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōkuninushi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōkuninushi}} *{{ill|lt=Sukunabikona|Q3275687|WD=Q3275687|lt=Sukunahikona-no-Mikoto|1=Sukunabikona}} *{{ill|lt=Kumano Gongen|Q11568925|WD=Q11568925|lt=Hayatama-no-o-no-Mikoto|1=Kumano Gongen}} === Merged enshrined deities === *{{ill|lt=Sarutahiko|Q3090037|WD=Q3090037|lt=Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto|1=Sarutahiko Ōkami}} *{{ill|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi|Q386563|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamazumi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōyamatsumi}} *{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Mizuwake-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} *{{ill|lt=Amaterasu|UNKNOWN|lt=Amaterasu-Ōmikami}} *{{ill|lt=Kukurihime|Q11619891|WD=Q11619891|lt=Kukurihime-no-Mikoto|1=Kukurihime}} *{{ill|lt=Emperor Nintoku|Q313119|WD=Q313119|lt=Ōsazaki-no-Mikoto|1=Emperor Nintoku}} *{{ill|lt=Kagutsuchi|Q1640658|WD=Q1640658|lt=Homusubi-no-Mikoto|1=Kagu-tsuchi}} *{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Homuda-wake-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}（{{ill|lt=Hachiman deity|Q261637|WD=Q261637|1=Hachiman}}）
