Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 5200005006637 == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '岐阜県加茂郡東白川村神土604', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:神田神社 (東白川村)]] {{wikidata link|Q11590647}} == Japanese content == {{神社 | 名称 = Kanda Shrine | 画像 = [[File:Kanda jinja Higashishirakawa.jpg|250px]]
Torii and haiden | 位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|38|37|N|137|19|25|E|display=title,inline}} | 所在地 = {{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|WD=Q131277|1=Gifu Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Kamo District, Gifu|Q1348656|WD=Q1348656|1=Kamo District, Gifu}}{{ill|lt=Higashishirakawa|Q1202765|WD=Q1202765|1=Higashishirakawa, Gifu}}{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kando|1=UNKNOWN}}604 | 祭神= | 社格= {{ill|lt=Shikinai Shosha|Q134917287|WD=Q134917287|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinai Shosha}}（small）・former {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}・{{ill|lt=Kinpei-sha|Q119929592|WD=Q119929592|1=Kinpei-sha}} | 別名 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Hakusan Myōdaigongen|1=UNKNOWN}}（{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Hakusan Myōri Gongen|1=UNKNOWN}}） | 創建= {{ill|lt=Yōrō|Q1373061|WD=Q1373061|1=Yōrō}} 2 | 例祭= late September | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Gifu|label=Kanda Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Kanda Shrine'''（かんだじんじゃ）is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kando|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|lt=Higashishirakawa|Q1202765|WD=Q1202765|1=Higashishirakawa, Gifu}}, {{ill|lt=Kamo District, Gifu|Q1348656|WD=Q1348656|1=Kamo District, Gifu}}, {{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|WD=Q131277|1=Gifu Prefecture}}. It is regarded as Kanda Shrine listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} for {{ill|lt=Mino Province|Q864156|WD=Q864156|1=Mino Province}} Kamo District. The annual grand festival in late September includes ritual ceremonies, lion dance, and ceremonial sake distribution. Floats used to be pulled through the village, but this stopped in the 1950s due to depopulation. In {{ill|lt=2008|Q2004|WD=Q2004|1=2008}}（{{ill|lt=Heisei|Q234510|WD=Q234510|1=Heisei era}} 20）, a new float was built and the procession revived. === Overview === It is said to have been founded in {{ill|lt=Yōrō|Q1373061|WD=Q1373061|1=Yōrō}} 2 ({{ill|lt=718|Q23534|WD=Q23534|1=718}}). In the Engishiki period, "Kanda" is said to have been read as "Kamuda." It declined after the mid-Heian period, but was restored in Kakei 2 ({{ill|lt=1388|Q6365|WD=Q6365|1=1388}}), and through {{ill|lt=Shinbutsu-shūgō|Q2279088|WD=Q2279088|1=Shinbutsu-shūgō}} was renamed '''Hakusan Myōdaigongen'''. It declined again in the Muromachi period, but was restored in Eiroku 1 ({{ill|lt=1558|Q6601|WD=Q6601|1=1558}}). Buildings were rebuilt in Kyōhō 2 ({{ill|lt=1717|Q6895|WD=Q6895|1=1717}}). In Meiji 1 ({{ill|lt=1868|Q7717|WD=Q7717|1=1868}}), this area underwent thorough {{ill|lt=haibutsu kishaku|Q702298|WD=Q702298|1=Haibutsu kishaku}} under the {{ill|lt=Naegi Domain|Q10863458|WD=Q10863458|1=Naegi Domain}}, and Hakusan Myōdaigongen was renamed Kanda Shrine. In Meiji 3 ({{ill|lt=1870|Q7741|WD=Q7741|1=1870}}), the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Department of Divinities|1=UNKNOWN}} recognized it as the Shikinaisha Kanda Shrine. In Meiji 4 ({{ill|lt=1871|Q7746|WD=Q7746|1=1871}}) it became a gōsha of Naegi Prefecture, and in Meiji 6 ({{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757|1=1873}}) a gōsha of Gifu Prefecture. On July 1, 1952 (Shōwa 27), it was designated by Gifu Prefectural Shrine Office as a shrine receiving regular offerings (Kinpei-sha designation No. 13){{sfn|木村|1997|p=121}}. === Enshrined deities === === Main deities === *{{ill|lt=Izanagi|Q813858|WD=Q813858|lt=Izanagi-no-Mikoto|1=Izanagi}} *{{ill|lt=Izanami|Q682306|WD=Q682306|lt=Izanami-no-Mikoto|1=Izanami}} *{{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōkuninushi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōkuninushi}} *{{ill|lt=Sukunabikona|Q3275687|WD=Q3275687|lt=Sukunahikona-no-Mikoto|1=Sukunabikona}} *{{ill|lt=Kumano Gongen|Q11568925|WD=Q11568925|lt=Hayatama-no-o-no-Mikoto|1=Kumano Gongen}} === Merged enshrined deities === *{{ill|lt=Sarutahiko|Q3090037|WD=Q3090037|lt=Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto|1=Sarutahiko Ōkami}} *{{ill|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi|Q386563|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamazumi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōyamatsumi}} *{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Mizuwake-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} *{{ill|lt=Amaterasu|UNKNOWN|lt=Amaterasu-Ōmikami}} *{{ill|lt=Kukurihime|Q11619891|WD=Q11619891|lt=Kukurihime-no-Mikoto|1=Kukurihime}} *{{ill|lt=Emperor Nintoku|Q313119|WD=Q313119|lt=Ōsazaki-no-Mikoto|1=Emperor Nintoku}} *{{ill|lt=Kagutsuchi|Q1640658|WD=Q1640658|lt=Homusubi-no-Mikoto|1=Kagu-tsuchi}} *{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Homuda-wake-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}（{{ill|lt=Hachiman deity|Q261637|WD=Q261637|1=Hachiman}}） *{{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} === Address === * Gifu Prefecture, Kamo District, Higashishirakawa Village, Kando 604 === Transportation === * {{ill|lt=Nōhi Bus|Q11566124|WD=Q11566124|lt=Nōhi Bus|1=Nohi Noriai Jidosha}} Shirakawa Line, get off at "{{ill|lt=Higashishirakawa Village Office|Q109368715|WD=Q109368715|1=Higashishirakawa Village Office}} Mae". ** From {{ill|lt=Shirakawa-guchi Station|Q5358687|WD=Q5358687|1=Shirakawaguchi Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Takayama Main Line|Q1188357|WD=Q1188357|1=Takayama Main Line}}, bus bound for Daimyōjin. === References === * {{Cite book |和書 |editor=Teru Kimura |title=Major Shrine Tour in Gifu Prefecture |publisher={{ill|lt=Nagaranoten Jinja|Q3335060|WD=Q3335060|1=Nagara Tenjin Shrine}} |date=1997 |ref={{SfnRef|木村|1997}}}} === External links === * [https://yaokami.jp/1212597/ Yaokami] === Related items === * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Koshihara Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}}[http://www.gifu-jinjacho.jp/syosai.php?shrno=522&shrname=%E2%96%A0%E8%B6%8A%E5%8E%9F%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE%E2%96%A0 Shrine Office] * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Goka Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} === Notes In the Engishiki period, "Kanda" is said to have been read as "Kamuda." It declined after the mid-Heian period, but was restored in Kakei 2 ({{ill|lt=1388|Q6365|WD=Q6365|1=1388}}), and through {{ill|lt=Shinbutsu-shūgō|Q2279088|WD=Q2279088|1=Shinbutsu-shūgō}} was renamed '''Hakusan Myōdaigongen'''. It declined again in the Muromachi period, but was restored in Eiroku 1 ({{ill|lt=1558|Q6601|WD=Q6601|1=1558}}). Buildings were rebuilt in Kyōhō 2 ({{ill|lt=1717|Q6895|WD=Q6895|1=1717}}). In Meiji 1 ({{ill|lt=1868|Q7717|WD=Q7717|1=1868}}), this area underwent thorough {{ill|lt=haibutsu kishaku|Q702298|WD=Q702298|1=Haibutsu kishaku}} under the {{ill|lt=Naegi Domain|Q10863458|WD=Q10863458|1=Naegi Domain}}, and Hakusan Myōdaigongen was renamed Kanda Shrine. In Meiji 3 ({{ill|lt=1870|Q7741|WD=Q7741|1=1870}}), the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Department of Divinities|1=UNKNOWN}} recognized it as the Shikinaisha Kanda Shrine. In Meiji 4 ({{ill|lt=1871|Q7746|WD=Q7746|1=1871}}) it became a gōsha of Naegi Prefecture, and in Meiji 6 ({{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757|1=1873}}) a gōsha of Gifu Prefecture. On July 1, 1952 (Shōwa 27), it was designated by Gifu Prefectural Shrine Office as a shrine receiving regular offerings (Kinpei-sha designation No. 13){{sfn|木村|1997|p=121}}. === Enshrined deities === === Main deities === *{{ill|lt=Izanagi|Q813858|WD=Q813858|lt=Izanagi-no-Mikoto|1=Izanagi}} *{{ill|lt=Izanami|Q682306|WD=Q682306|lt=Izanami-no-Mikoto|1=Izanami}} *{{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōkuninushi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōkuninushi}} *{{ill|lt=Sukunabikona|Q3275687|WD=Q3275687|lt=Sukunahikona-no-Mikoto|1=Sukunabikona}} *{{ill|lt=Kumano Gongen|Q11568925|WD=Q11568925|lt=Hayatama-no-o-no-Mikoto|1=Kumano Gongen}} === Merged enshrined deities === *{{ill|lt=Sarutahiko|Q3090037|WD=Q3090037|lt=Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto|1=Sarutahiko Ōkami}} *{{ill|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi|Q386563|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamazumi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōyamatsumi}} *{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Mizuwake-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} *{{ill|lt=Amaterasu|UNKNOWN|lt=Amaterasu-Ōmikami}} *{{ill|lt=Kukurihime|Q11619891|WD=Q11619891|lt=Kukurihime-no-Mikoto|1=Kukurihime}} *{{ill|lt=Emperor Nintoku|Q313119|WD=Q313119|lt=Ōsazaki-no-Mikoto|1=Emperor Nintoku}} *{{ill|lt=Kagutsuchi|Q1640658|WD=Q1640658|lt=Homusubi-no-Mikoto|1=Kagu-tsuchi}} *{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Homuda-wake-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}（{{ill|lt=Hachiman deity|Q261637|WD=Q261637|1=Hachiman}}） *{{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} === Address === * Gifu Prefecture, Kamo District, Higashishirakawa Village, Kando 604 === Transportation === * Office}} Mae". ** From {{ill|lt=Shirakawa-guchi Station|Q5358687|WD=Q5358687|1=Shirakawaguchi Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Takayama Main Line|Q1188357|WD=Q1188357|1=Takayama Main Line}}, bus bound for Daimyōjin. === References === * {{Cite book |和書 |editor=Teru Kimura |title=Major Shrine Tour in Gifu Prefecture |publisher={{ill|lt=Nagaranoten Jinja|Q3335060|WD=Q3335060|1=Nagara Tenjin Shrine}} {{神社 | 名称 = Kanda Shrine | 画像 = [[File:Kanda jinja Higashishirakawa.jpg|250px]]
Torii and haiden | 位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|38|37|N|137|19|25|E|display=title,inline}} | 所在地 = {{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|qid=Q131277|1=Gifu Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Kamo District, Gifu|Q1348656|qid=Q1348656|1=Kamo District, Gifu}}{{ill|lt=Higashishirakawa|Q1202765|qid=Q1202765|1=Higashishirakawa, Gifu}}{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kando|1=UNKNOWN}}604 | 祭神= | 社格= {{ill|lt=Shikinai Shosha|Q134917287|qid=Q134917287|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinai Shosha}}（small）・former {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|qid=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}・{{ill|lt=Kinpei-sha|Q119929592|qid=Q119929592|1=Kinpei-sha}} | 別名 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Hakusan Myōdaigongen|1=UNKNOWN}}（{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Hakusan Myōri Gongen|1=UNKNOWN}}） | 創建= {{ill|lt=Yōrō|Q1373061|qid=Q1373061|1=Yōrō}} 2 | 例祭= late September | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Gifu|label=Kanda Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Kanda Shrine'''（かんだじんじゃ）is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kando|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|lt=Higashishirakawa|Q1202765|qid=Q1202765|1=Higashishirakawa, Gifu}}, {{ill|lt=Kamo District, Gifu|Q1348656|qid=Q1348656|1=Kamo District, Gifu}}, {{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|qid=Q131277|1=Gifu Prefecture}}. It is regarded as Kanda Shrine listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|qid=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} for {{ill|lt=Mino Province|Q864156|qid=Q864156|1=Mino Province}} Kamo District. The annual grand festival in late September includes ritual ceremonies, lion dance, and ceremonial sake distribution. Floats used to be pulled through the village, but this stopped in the 1950s due to depopulation. In {{ill|lt=2008|Q2004|qid=Q2004|1=2008}}（{{ill|lt=Heisei|Q234510|qid=Q234510|1=Heisei era}} 20）, a new float was built and the procession revived. === Overview === It is said to have been founded in {{ill|lt=Yōrō|Q1373061|qid=Q1373061|1=Yōrō}} 2 ({{ill|lt=718|Q23534|qid=Q23534|1=718}}). In the Engishiki period, "Kanda" is said to have been read as "Kamuda." It declined after the mid-Heian period, but was restored in Kakei 2 ({{ill|lt=1388|Q6365|qid=Q6365|1=1388}}), and through {{ill|lt=Shinbutsu-shūgō|Q2279088|qid=Q2279088|1=Shinbutsu-shūgō}} was renamed '''Hakusan Myōdaigongen'''. It declined again in the Muromachi period, but was restored in Eiroku 1 ({{ill|lt=1558|Q6601|qid=Q6601|1=1558}}). Buildings were rebuilt in Kyōhō 2 ({{ill|lt=1717|Q6895|qid=Q6895|1=1717}}). In Meiji 1 ({{ill|lt=1868|Q7717|qid=Q7717|1=1868}}), this area underwent thorough {{ill|lt=haibutsu kishaku|Q702298|qid=Q702298|1=Haibutsu kishaku}} under the {{ill|lt=Naegi Domain|Q10863458|qid=Q10863458|1=Naegi Domain}}, and Hakusan Myōdaigongen was renamed Kanda Shrine. In Meiji 3 ({{ill|lt=1870|Q7741|qid=Q7741|1=1870}}), the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Department of Divinities|1=UNKNOWN}} recognized it as the Shikinaisha Kanda Shrine. In Meiji 4 ({{ill|lt=1871|Q7746|qid=Q7746|1=1871}}) it became a gōsha of Naegi Prefecture, and in Meiji 6 ({{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|qid=Q7757|1=1873}}) a gōsha of Gifu Prefecture. On July 1, 1952 (Shōwa 27), it was designated by Gifu Prefectural Shrine Office as a shrine receiving regular offerings (Kinpei-sha designation No. 13){{sfn|木村|1997|p=121}}. === Enshrined deities === === Main deities === *{{ill|lt=Izanagi|Q813858|qid=Q813858|lt=Izanagi-no-Mikoto|1=Izanagi}} *{{ill|lt=Izanami|Q682306|qid=Q682306|lt=Izanami-no-Mikoto|1=Izanami}} *{{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|qid=Q276944|lt=Ōkuninushi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōkuninushi}} *{{ill|lt=Sukunabikona|Q3275687|qid=Q3275687|lt=Sukunahikona-no-Mikoto|1=Sukunabikona}} *{{ill|lt=Kumano Gongen|Q11568925|qid=Q11568925|lt=Hayatama-no-o-no-Mikoto|1=Kumano Gongen}} === Merged enshrined deities === *{{ill|lt=Sarutahiko|Q3090037|qid=Q3090037|lt=Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto|1=Sarutahiko Ōkami}} *{{ill|qid=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi|Q386563|qid=Q386563|lt=Ōyamazumi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōyamatsumi}} *{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Mizuwake-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} *{{ill|lt=Amaterasu|UNKNOWN|lt=Amaterasu-Ōmikami}} *{{ill|lt=Kukurihime|Q11619891|qid=Q11619891|lt=Kukurihime-no-Mikoto|1=Kukurihime}} *{{ill|lt=Emperor Nintoku|Q313119|qid=Q313119|lt=Ōsazaki-no-Mikoto|1=Emperor Nintoku}} *{{ill|lt=Kagutsuchi|Q1640658|qid=Q1640658|lt=Homusubi-no-Mikoto|1=Kagu-tsuchi}} *{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Homuda-wake-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}（{{ill|lt=Hachiman deity|Q261637|qid=Q261637|1=Hachiman}}） Office}} Mae". ** From {{ill|lt=Shirakawa-guchi Station|Q5358687|qid=Q5358687|1=Shirakawaguchi Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Takayama Main Line|Q1188357|qid=Q1188357|1=Takayama Main Line}}, bus bound for {{神社 | 名称 = Kanda Shrine | 画像 = [[File:Kanda jinja Higashishirakawa.jpg|250px]]
Torii and haiden | 位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|38|37|N|137|19|25|E|display=title,inline}} | 所在地 = {{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|qid=Q131277|1=Gifu Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Kamo District, Gifu|Q1348656|qid=Q1348656|1=Kamo District, Gifu}}{{ill|lt=Higashishirakawa|Q1202765|qid=Q1202765|1=Higashishirakawa, Gifu}}{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kando|1=UNKNOWN}}604 | 祭神= | 社格= {{ill|lt=Shikinai Shosha|Q134917287|qid=Q134917287|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinai Shosha}}（small）・former {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|qid=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}・{{ill|lt=Kinpei-sha|Q119929592|qid=Q119929592|1=Kinpei-sha}} | 別名 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Hakusan Myōdaigongen|1=UNKNOWN}}（{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Hakusan Myōri Gongen|1=UNKNOWN}}） | 創建= {{ill|lt=Yōrō|Q1373061|qid=Q1373061|1=Yōrō}} 2 | 例祭= late September | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Gifu|label=Kanda Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Kanda Shrine'''（かんだじんじゃ）is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kando|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|lt=Higashishirakawa|Q1202765|qid=Q1202765|1=Higashishirakawa, Gifu}}, {{ill|lt=Kamo District, Gifu|Q1348656|qid=Q1348656|1=Kamo District, Gifu}}, {{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|qid=Q131277|1=Gifu Prefecture}}. It is regarded as Kanda Shrine listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|qid=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} for {{ill|lt=Mino Province|Q864156|qid=Q864156|1=Mino Province}} Kamo District. The annual grand festival in late September includes ritual ceremonies, lion dance, and ceremonial sake distribution. Floats used to be pulled through the village, but this stopped in the 1950s due to depopulation. In {{ill|lt=2008|Q2004|qid=Q2004|1=2008}}（{{ill|lt=Heisei|Q234510|qid=Q234510|1=Heisei era}} 20）, a new float was built and the procession revived. === Overview === It is said to have been founded in {{ill|lt=Yōrō|Q1373061|qid=Q1373061|1=Yōrō}} 2 ({{ill|lt=718|Q23534|qid=Q23534|1=718}}). In the Engishiki period, "Kanda" is said to have been read as "Kamuda." It declined after the mid-Heian period, but was restored in Kakei 2 ({{ill|lt=1388|Q6365|qid=Q6365|1=1388}}), and through {{ill|lt=Shinbutsu-shūgō|Q2279088|qid=Q2279088|1=Shinbutsu-shūgō}} was renamed '''Hakusan Myōdaigongen'''. It declined again in the Muromachi period, but was restored in Eiroku 1 ({{ill|lt=1558|Q6601|qid=Q6601|1=1558}}). Buildings were rebuilt in Kyōhō 2 ({{ill|lt=1717|Q6895|qid=Q6895|1=1717}}). In Meiji 1 ({{ill|lt=1868|Q7717|qid=Q7717|1=1868}}), this area underwent thorough {{ill|lt=haibutsu kishaku|Q702298|qid=Q702298|1=Haibutsu kishaku}} under the {{ill|lt=Naegi Domain|Q10863458|qid=Q10863458|1=Naegi Domain}}, and Hakusan Myōdaigongen was renamed Kanda Shrine. In Meiji 3 ({{ill|lt=1870|Q7741|qid=Q7741|1=1870}}), the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Department of Divinities|1=UNKNOWN}} recognized it as the Shikinaisha Kanda Shrine. In Meiji 4 ({{ill|lt=1871|Q7746|qid=Q7746|1=1871}}) it became a gōsha of Naegi Prefecture, and in Meiji 6 ({{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|qid=Q7757|1=1873}}) a gōsha of Gifu Prefecture. On July 1, 1952 (Shōwa 27), it was designated by Gifu Prefectural Shrine Office as a shrine receiving regular offerings (Kinpei-sha designation No. 13){{sfn|木村|1997|p=121}}. === Enshrined deities === === Main deities === *{{ill|lt=Izanagi|Q813858|qid=Q813858|lt=Izanagi-no-Mikoto|1=Izanagi}} *{{ill|lt=Izanami|Q682306|qid=Q682306|lt=Izanami-no-Mikoto|1=Izanami}} *{{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|qid=Q276944|lt=Ōkuninushi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōkuninushi}} *{{ill|lt=Sukunabikona|Q3275687|qid=Q3275687|lt=Sukunahikona-no-Mikoto|1=Sukunabikona}} *{{ill|lt=Kumano Gongen|Q11568925|qid=Q11568925|lt=Hayatama-no-o-no-Mikoto|1=Kumano Gongen}} === Merged enshrined deities === *{{ill|lt=Sarutahiko|Q3090037|qid=Q3090037|lt=Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto|1=Sarutahiko Ōkami}} *{{ill|qid=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi|Q386563|qid=Q386563|lt=Ōyamazumi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōyamatsumi}} *{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Mizuwake-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} *{{ill|lt=Amaterasu|UNKNOWN|lt=Amaterasu-Ōmikami}} *{{ill|lt=Kikurihime|Q11619891|qid=Q11619891|lt=Kikurihime-no-Mikoto|1=Kikurihime}} *{{ill|lt=Emperor Nintoku|Q313119|qid=Q313119|lt=Ōsazaki-no-Mikoto|1=Emperor Nintoku}} *{{ill|lt=Kagutsuchi|Q1640658|qid=Q1640658|lt=Homusubi-no-Mikoto|1=Kagu-tsuchi}} *{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Homuda-wake-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}（{{ill|lt=Hachiman deity|Q261637|qid=Q261637|1=Hachiman}}） *{{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|qid=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} === Address === * Gifu Prefecture, Kamo District, Higashishirakawa Village, Kando 604 === Transportation === * Office}} Mae". ** From {{ill|lt=Shirakawa-guchi Station|Q5358687|qid=Q5358687|1=Shirakawaguchi Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Takayama Main Line|Q1188357|qid=Q1188357|1=Takayama Main Line}}, bus bound for shrine ranking (P13723) == * {{ill|Gō-sha|lt=Gō-sha|ja|郷社|qid=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} ** End time: {'time': '+1946-02-02T00:00:00Z', 'timezone': 0, 'before': 0, 'after': 0, 'precision': 11, 'calendarmodel': 'http://www.wikidata.org/entity/Q1985727'} ** P1534: {{ill|Q3029647|ltq=Q3029647|qid=Q3029647|1=Shinto Directive}} == country (P17) == * {{ill|Q17|ltq=Q17|qid=Q17|1=Japan}} == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 5200005006637 == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '岐阜県加茂郡東白川村神土604', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:神田神社 (東白川村)]] {{wikidata link|Q11590647}} == Japanese content == {{神社 | 名称 = Kanda Shrine | 画像 = [[File:Kanda jinja Higashishirakawa.jpg|250px]]
Torii and haiden | 位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|38|37|N|137|19|25|E|display=title,inline}} | 所在地 = {{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|qid=Q131277|1=Gifu Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Kamo District, Gifu|Q1348656|qid=Q1348656|1=Kamo District, Gifu}}{{ill|lt=Higashishirakawa|Q1202765|qid=Q1202765|1=Higashishirakawa, Gifu}}{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kando|1=UNKNOWN}}604 | 祭神= | 社格= {{ill|lt=Shikinai Shosha|Q134917287|qid=Q134917287|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinai Shosha}}（small）・former {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|qid=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}・{{ill|lt=Kinpei-sha|Q119929592|qid=Q119929592|1=Kinpei-sha}} | 別名 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Hakusan Myōdaigongen|1=UNKNOWN}}（{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Hakusan Myōri Gongen|1=UNKNOWN}}） | 創建= {{ill|lt=Yōrō|Q1373061|qid=Q1373061|1=Yōrō}} 2 | 例祭= late September | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Gifu|label=Kanda Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Kanda Shrine'''（かんだじんじゃ）is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kando|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|lt=Higashishirakawa|Q1202765|qid=Q1202765|1=Higashishirakawa, Gifu}}, {{ill|lt=Kamo District, Gifu|Q1348656|qid=Q1348656|1=Kamo District, Gifu}}, {{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|qid=Q131277|1=Gifu Prefecture}}. It is regarded as Kanda Shrine listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|qid=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} for {{ill|lt=Mino Province|Q864156|qid=Q864156|1=Mino Province}} Kamo District. The annual grand festival in late September includes ritual ceremonies, lion dance, and ceremonial sake distribution. Floats used to be pulled through the village, but this stopped in the 1950s due to depopulation. In {{ill|lt=2008|Q2004|qid=Q2004|1=2008}}（{{ill|lt=Heisei|Q234510|qid=Q234510|1=Heisei era}} 20）, a new float was built and the procession revived. === Overview === It is said to have been founded in {{ill|lt=Yōrō|Q1373061|qid=Q1373061|1=Yōrō}} 2 ({{ill|lt=718|Q23534|qid=Q23534|1=718}}). In the Engishiki period, "Kanda" is said to have been read as "Kamuda." It declined after the mid-Heian period, but was restored in Kakei 2 ({{ill|lt=1388|Q6365|qid=Q6365|1=1388}}), and through {{ill|lt=Shinbutsu-shūgō|Q2279088|qid=Q2279088|1=Shinbutsu-shūgō}} was renamed '''Hakusan Myōdaigongen'''. It declined again in the Muromachi period, but was restored in Eiroku 1 ({{ill|lt=1558|Q6601|qid=Q6601|1=1558}}). Buildings were rebuilt in Kyōhō 2 ({{ill|lt=1717|Q6895|qid=Q6895|1=1717}}). In Meiji 1 ({{ill|lt=1868|Q7717|qid=Q7717|1=1868}}), this area underwent thorough {{ill|lt=haibutsu kishaku|Q702298|qid=Q702298|1=Haibutsu kishaku}} under the {{ill|lt=Naegi Domain|Q10863458|qid=Q10863458|1=Naegi Domain}}, and Hakusan Myōdaigongen was renamed Kanda Shrine. In Meiji 3 ({{ill|lt=1870|Q7741|qid=Q7741|1=1870}}), the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Department of Divinities|1=UNKNOWN}} recognized it as the Shikinaisha Kanda Shrine. In Meiji 4 ({{ill|lt=1871|Q7746|qid=Q7746|1=1871}}) it became a gōsha of Naegi Prefecture, and in Meiji 6 ({{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|qid=Q7757|1=1873}}) a gōsha of Gifu Prefecture. On July 1, 1952 (Shōwa 27), it was designated by Gifu Prefectural Shrine Office as a shrine receiving regular offerings (Kinpei-sha designation No. 13){{sfn|木村|1997|p=121}}. === Enshrined deities === === Main deities === *{{ill|lt=Izanagi|Q813858|qid=Q813858|lt=Izanagi-no-Mikoto|1=Izanagi}} *{{ill|lt=Izanami|Q682306|qid=Q682306|lt=Izanami-no-Mikoto|1=Izanami}} *{{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|qid=Q276944|lt=Ōkuninushi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōkuninushi}} *{{ill|lt=Sukunabikona|Q3275687|qid=Q3275687|lt=Sukunahikona-no-Mikoto|1=Sukunabikona}} *{{ill|lt=Kumano Gongen|Q11568925|qid=Q11568925|lt=Hayatama-no-o-no-Mikoto|1=Kumano Gongen}} === Merged enshrined deities === *{{ill|lt=Sarutahiko|Q3090037|qid=Q3090037|lt=Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto|1=Sarutahiko Ōkami}} *{{ill|qid=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi|Q386563|qid=Q386563|lt=Ōyamazumi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōyamatsumi}} *{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Mizuwake-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} *{{ill|lt=Amaterasu|UNKNOWN|lt=Amaterasu-Ōmikami}} *{{ill|lt=Kikurihime|Q11619891|qid=Q11619891|lt=Kikurihime-no-Mikoto|1=Kikurihime}} *{{ill|lt=Emperor Nintoku|Q313119|qid=Q313119|lt=Ōsazaki-no-Mikoto|1=Emperor Nintoku}} *{{ill|lt=Kagutsuchi|Q1640658|qid=Q1640658|lt=Homusubi-no-Mikoto|1=Kagu-tsuchi}} *{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Homuda-wake-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}（{{ill|lt=Hachiman deity|Q261637|qid=Q261637|1=Hachiman}}） *{{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|qid=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} === Address === * Gifu Prefecture, Kamo District, Higashishirakawa Village, Kando 604 === Transportation === * {{ill|lt=Nōhi Bus|Q11566124|qid=Q11566124|lt=Nōhi Bus|1=Nohi Noriai Jidosha}} Shirakawa Line, get off at "{{ill|lt=Higashishirakawa Village Office|Q109368715|qid=Q109368715|1=Higashishirakawa Village Office}} Mae". ** From {{ill|lt=Shirakawa-guchi Station|Q5358687|qid=Q5358687|1=Shirakawaguchi Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Takayama Main Line|Q1188357|qid=Q1188357|1=Takayama Main Line}}, bus bound for Daimyōjin. === References === * {{Cite book |和書 |editor=Teru Kimura |title=Major Shrine Tour in Gifu Prefecture |publisher={{ill|lt=Nagaranoten Jinja|Q3335060|qid=Q3335060|1=Nagara Tenjin Shrine}} |date=1997 |ref={{SfnRef|木村|1997}}}} === External links === * [https://yaokami.jp/1212597/ Yaokami] === Related items === * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Koshihara Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}}[http://www.gifu-jinjacho.jp/syosai.php?shrno=522&shrname=%E2%96%A0%E8%B6%8A%E5%8E%9F%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE%E2%96%A0 Shrine Office] * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Goka Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} === Notes
